ID:       ddx-424fdac4
Title:    Ratify DDx specs for Fizeau-owned routing and no-agent cleanup
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:docs, area:spec, area:fizeau, kind:design, breaking, decomposed
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-f088a3fd
Deps:     ddx-e2586179, ddx-242d2781, ddx-55559451, ddx-119ddb10, ddx-b42d73be
Desc:     PROBLEM
Implementation agents need durable specs before removing `ddx agent`, renaming DDx internals, or consuming Fizeau contract changes. Today the specs still mix the old `agent` vocabulary with the desired DDx/Fizeau boundary, so agents can reasonably follow stale instructions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 still describes an `Agent Passthrough` structural mount for `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still describes the upstream invocation boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` and `DDx Agent Service` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:1-23. FEAT-010 still says layer 1 consumes upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. TP-020 remains a historical DDx-side routing test plan even though Fizeau owns routing. docs/migrations/routing-config.md:1-13 removes some old routing keys but does not yet document the full `agent.*` removal / Fizeau-vs-execution config split.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the specs before implementation. Required docs: FEAT-001, FEAT-006 or its renamed replacement, FEAT-010, ADR-024, TP-020, docs/migrations/routing-config.md, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md, and DDx skill references. The specs must state: DDx owns task execution (`run`, `try`, `work`); Fizeau owns routing, provider/model discovery, native transcript/session rendering, and fuzzy model constraint resolution; `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are explicit operator passthrough only; DDx passes raw strings unchanged; `ddx agent` is removed without deprecation; and `agent` is allowed only for actual external AI agent concepts.

NON-SCOPE
No production code removal in this bead. No Fizeau implementation work. No compatibility alias design. No generated CLI docs rebuild unless the docs generator is part of the spec-doc workflow.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz` contract identity
- fizeau-400b57fe for Fizeau-owned model alias/fuzzy matching

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-f088a3fd. Dependencies: none; this bead comes first.
Accept:   1. FEAT-001 removes the `ddx agent` structural mount and defines only `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and any Fizeau diagnostics namespace that remains, with no aliases.
2. FEAT-006 is renamed or rewritten as the DDx consumer spec for Fizeau, and no longer presents `agentlib.DdxAgent` / `ddx-agent` as the desired contract name.
3. FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.
4. ADR-024 says escalation changes only MinPower/MaxPower/request facts and Fizeau chooses the concrete route.
5. TP-020 is replaced or superseded with a DDx boundary test plan proving DDx does not normalize, fuzzy-match, or route models.
6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md documents removal of `agent.*` defaults and the split between Fizeau routing config and DDx execution config.
7. DDx skill references explain explicit passthrough and Fizeau-owned model matching using examples such as `--model qwen36`, while stating DDx passes the raw string unchanged.
8. `rg -n "ddx agent|ddx-agent|agentlib.DdxAgent|Agent Service|cli/internal/agent" docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited historical or literal-external-agent hits documented in the change.
9. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

ID:       ddx-ddacd4ff
Title:    C14: delete ExecuteBead* shims and aliases; verify all callers ported
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:cleanup
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-387a0178, ddx-3ee4ddcf
Desc:     PROBLEM
After the C13 refactor (ddx-387a0178), shim typedefs of the form ExecuteBeadWorker, ExecuteBeadReport, ExecuteBeadResult, ExecuteBeadStatus, ExecuteBeadOutcome remain in the codebase as temporary aliases. These shims prevent callers from adopting the canonical names and leave dead identifier surface that grep/tooling cannot distinguish from active usage.

ROOT CAUSE
C13 (ddx-387a0178) introduces the canonical type names as part of the file/identifier rename step and adds shim typedefs for backward compat during the migration window. The shim typedefs live in the files renamed by C13 (exact file:line determined after C13 lands). The CLI aliases ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead continue to register live cobra commands after C13, pointing at logic now accessible via ddx work and ddx try.

PROPOSED FIX
- Sweep all callsites off the shim names (grep for ExecuteBeadWorker | ExecuteBeadReport | ExecuteBeadResult | ExecuteBead\b | ExecuteBeadStatus | ExecuteBeadOutcome in cli/).
- Delete the shim typedefs from C13.
- Remove cobra command registrations for ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead; replace with a cobra RunE that prints a deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
- Verify: grep returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test files.

NON-SCOPE
- Deleting the entire ddx agent command group (that is ddx-d8474e0e, a separate bead).
- Changing execute-loop/try behavior.
Accept:   1. grep -rn 'ExecuteBeadWorker\|ExecuteBeadReport\|ExecuteBeadResult\|ExecuteBead\b\|ExecuteBeadStatus\|ExecuteBeadOutcome' cli/ returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test source files.
2. ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead cobra commands replaced with deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
3. TestNoBuildRegressions_AfterShimDelete: cd cli && go build ./... green.
4. cd cli && go test ./... green.
5. Server worker integration test green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
7. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.RecoverOrphans (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:383) is invoked from the ddx try / ddx work code path. Today it is called ONLY from cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:168 across the entire CLI tree (verified by grep). If it is not yet wired into the try/work path, lift the call into ExecuteBeadWithConfig (engine) or into try.Attempt before this bead's cobra-replacement step. A regression test (TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn, in cli/cmd/try_test.go) must assert the behavior survives.
8. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.BuildLandingGateContext (cli/internal/agent/landing_gate_context.go:28) is invoked when landing through the ddx try CLI path. Today it is called from cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:206 and from cli/internal/server/workers.go:1700 (server-side worker), but NOT from the CLI try/work path (verified by grep). If not wired through, lift into the landing helper used by try.Attempt. A regression test (TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding, in cli/cmd/try_test.go) must assert the behavior survives.
9. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService preflight (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:390, called at cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:160-164 when no AgentRunnerOverride) is either invoked from ddx try / ddx work (CLI) or explicitly documented as N/A in this bead's notes with rationale. Today try.go does not call it.
10. INTERSECTIONS: ddx-d8474e0e (full ddx agent retirement) reuses the same engine wiring; the lifts in AC #7-9 are prerequisites for both this bead and ddx-d8474e0e. ddx-a1ae37ff (commit-scoping --no-verify fix) is independent and lands first.

20		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
21	)
22	
23	// Exit codes for ddx try terminal outcomes. These are specific to ddx try
24	// and intentionally do not reuse the generic ExitCode* constants because
25	// the bead-execution outcome vocabulary is distinct from generic CLI errors.
26	const (
27		tryExitSuccess         = 0 // success or already_satisfied
28		tryExitPreservedOrNone = 1 // preserved_for_review or no_changes
29		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
30	)
31	
32	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
33	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
34	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
35	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
36	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
37		cmd := &cobra.Command{
38			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
39			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
40			SilenceUsage: true,
41			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
42	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
43	
44	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
45	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
46	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
47	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
48	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
49	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
50	
51	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
52	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
53	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
54	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
55	
56	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
57	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
58	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
59	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
60	
61	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
62	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
63	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
64	
65	Exit codes:
66	  0  success or already_satisfied
67	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
68	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
69	`,
70			Example: `  # Execute a specific bead
71	  ddx try ddx-abc12345
72	
73	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
74	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini
75	
76	  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
77	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing`,
78			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
79			RunE: f.runTry,
80		}
81	
82		// Routing knobs — identical to "ddx work" passthrough surface.
83		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
84		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
85		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
86		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
87		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
89		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
90		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
91		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
92		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
93		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
94		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
95		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
98	
99		return cmd
100	}

102	// runTry is the RunE for "ddx try <bead-id>".
103	func (f *CommandFactory) runTry(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
104		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
105			return err
106		}
107	
108		beadID := args[0]
109	
110		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
111		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
112	
113		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
114		// Runs before claim/worktree setup so the operator sees the warning before
115		// any bead state changes.
116		preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
117		f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
118			emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
119		})
120	
121		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
122		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
123		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
124		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
125		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
126		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
127		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
128		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
129		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
130		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
131		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
132		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
133		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
134		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
135	
136		if noReview && !noReviewAck {
137			return fmt.Errorf("--no-review requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing (break-glass acknowledgement)")
138		}
139	
140		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
141	
142		// Pre-flight: look up the bead.
143		target, err := store.Get(beadID)
144		if err != nil {
145			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead not found: %s\n", beadID)
146			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
147		}
148	
149		// Pre-flight: verify the bead is in a claimable state.
150		// Claimable = open, or stale in_progress (the loop handles stale reclaim).
151		switch target.Status {
152		case bead.StatusOpen, bead.StatusInProgress:
153			// allowed — loop's Claim() handles stale-heartbeat reclaim
154		default:
155			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not claimable (status=%s)\n", target.Status)
156			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
157		}
158	
159		// Pre-flight: check for unmet dependencies.
160		if unmet := unmetDeps(store, target); len(unmet) > 0 {
161			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead has unmet dependencies: %s\n", strings.Join(unmet, ", "))
162			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
163		}
164	
165		// Wrap the real store so that ReadyExecution() returns only the target bead.
166		// All other operations pass through to the underlying store unchanged.
167		forcedStore := &singleBeadStore{Store: store, target: *target}
168	
169		// Set up progress logging (same as runAgentExecuteLoopImpl).
170		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
171		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
172		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
173		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
174		var loopSink io.Writer
175		if lf, openErr := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); openErr == nil {
176			loopSink = lf
177			defer lf.Close() //nolint:errcheck
178		}
179	
180		// Worker-probe (best-effort server mirror; non-blocking).
181		probeIdent := workerprobe.Identity{
182			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
183			Harness:      harness,
184			Model:        model,
185			ExecutorPID:  os.Getpid(),
186			ExecutorHost: hostnameOrEmpty(),
187			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
188		}
189		probe := workerprobe.New(probeIdent, workerprobe.Config{
190			AddrFunc: serverpkg.ReadServerAddr,
191		})
192		probeCtx, probeCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
193		probe.Start(probeCtx)
194		defer func() {
195			probeCancel()
196			probe.Stop()
197		}()
198		if loopSink != nil {
199			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(loopSink, probe)
200		} else {
201			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(io.Discard, probe)
202		}
203	
204		// Process-local LandCoordinator.
205		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
206		defer localCoord.Stop()
207	
208		// Post-merge reviewer (on by default, skipped via --no-review).
209		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
210		if !noReview {
211			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
212				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
213				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
214				BeadEvents:  bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
215				Harness:     reviewHarness,
216				Model:       reviewModel,
217			}
218		}
219	
220		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
221	
222		// Determine whether zero-config auto-route applies (same logic as runWork).
223		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
224			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
225		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
226		resourceChecker := buildCLIResourceChecker(projectRoot, f.resourceCheckerOverride)
227	
228		// Build the executor: either the test override or the real single-tier executor.
229		var executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
230		if f.tryExecutorOverride != nil {
231			executor = f.tryExecutorOverride
232		} else {
233			executor = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, execBeadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
234				var inferredTier escalation.ModelTier
235				if autoInferTier {
236					if b, getErr := store.Get(execBeadID); getErr == nil {
237						inferredTier = escalation.InferTier(b)
238					} else {
239						inferredTier = escalation.TierCheap
240					}
241				}
242	
243				loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{
244					Harness:           harness,
245					Model:             model,
246					Provider:          provider,
247					ModelRef:          modelRef,
248					Profile:           profile,
249					Effort:            effort,
250					MinPower:          minPower,
251					MaxPower:          maxPower,
252					OpaquePassthrough: true, // ddx try treats flags as opaque passthrough
253				}
254				if requestTimeout > 0 {
255					loopOverrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
256				}
257				attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, loopOverrides)
258				gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
259				res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, execBeadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
260					FromRev:         fromRev,
261					Output:          cmd.OutOrStdout(),
262					BeadEvents:      bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
263					ResourceChecker: resourceChecker,
264				}, gitOps)
265				if execErr != nil && res == nil {
266					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
267				}
268				if res != nil && agent.IsResourceExhaustedStatus(res.Status) {
269					return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
270				}
271				if execErr != nil {
272					agent.MarkResultExecutionError(res, execErr)
273					return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
274				}
275				if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
276					targetBead, _ := store.Get(execBeadID)
277					landRes, _, landErr := agent.SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
278						ctx, projectRoot, targetBead, res,
279						func(req agent.LandRequest) (*agent.LandResult, error) { return localCoord.Submit(req) },
280						bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
281						resolveClaimAssignee(), "ddx try",
282						nil,
283					)
284					if landErr == nil {
285						agent.ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res, landRes)
286						_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
287					} else {
288						agent.MarkResultLandError(projectRoot, res, landErr)
289						_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
290						return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
291					}
292				} else if res != nil && (res.Outcome == agent.ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed || res.ExitCode != 0) {
293					if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
294						res.Outcome = "preserved"
295					} else {
296						res.Outcome = "error"
297					}
298					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
299				} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
300					res.Outcome = "no-changes"
301					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
302				}
303				tierStr := ""
304				if inferredTier != "" {
305					tierStr = string(inferredTier)
306				}
307				return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, tierStr), nil
308			})
309		}
310	
311		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
312			Store:    forcedStore,
313			Executor: executor,
314			Reviewer: reviewer,
315		}
316	
317		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
318			Assignee: resolveClaimAssignee(),
319			Harness:  harness,
320			Model:    model,
321			Provider: provider,
322			ModelRef: modelRef,
323			Profile:  profile,
324			Effort:   effort,
325		}
326		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
327		if err != nil {
328			return fmt.Errorf("load resolved config: %w", err)
329		}
330		if _, err := resourceChecker.Check(cmd.Context()); err != nil {
331			if resErr, ok := err.(*agent.ResourceExhaustedError); ok && resErr != nil {
332				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), resErr.Error())
333			}
334			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: err.Error()}
335		}
336	
337		cliLandingOps := agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
338		var qualityRunner agent.AgentRunner
339		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
340			qualityRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
341		}
342		lintHook := agent.NewPreDispatchLintHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner)
343		triageHook := agent.NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner, nil)
344		result, runErr := worker.Run(cmd.Context(), rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
345			Mode:                  executeloop.ModeOnce,
346			Log:                   cmd.OutOrStdout(),
347			EventSink:             loopSink,
348			WorkerID:              resolveClaimAssignee(),
349			ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
350			ResourceChecker:       resourceChecker,
351			SessionID:             loopSessionID,
352			PreClaimHook:          buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps),
353			PreDispatchLintHook:   lintHook,
354			PostAttemptTriageHook: triageHook,
355			NoReview:              noReview,
356		})
357		if runErr != nil {
358			if (errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(runErr, context.DeadlineExceeded)) && result != nil && len(result.Results) > 0 {
359				writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.Results[0])
360			}
361			return runErr
362		}
363	
364		// Report outcome.
365		if result.NoReadyWork {
366			// Bead was not in the execution-ready queue even after pre-flight passed.
367			// This can happen if a heartbeat re-appeared just before claim, making it
368			// not stale-enough for reclaim. Surface a clear message.
369			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not execution-ready (it may be actively claimed by another worker): %s\n", beadID)
370			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
371		}
372	
373		if len(result.Results) == 0 {
374			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
375		}
376		report := result.Results[0]
377		writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), report)
378		if intent := escalation.ParseExecutionHint(target); intent.Source == escalation.ExecutionIntentSourceBeadHint && intent.RequestedTier != "" {
379			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "routing intent: tier=%s source=%s\n", intent.RequestedTier, intent.Source)
380		}
381	
382		return tryExitCodeForStatus(report.Status)
383	}
384	
385	func writeTryResult(w io.Writer, report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
386		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nbead: %s\nstatus: %s\n", report.BeadID, report.Status)
387		if report.Detail != "" {
388			fmt.Fprintf(w, "detail: %s\n", report.Detail)
389		}
390		if report.ResultRev != "" {
391			fmt.Fprintf(w, "result_rev: %s\n", report.ResultRev)
392		}
393		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
394			fmt.Fprintf(w, "preserve_ref: %s\n", report.PreserveRef)
395		}
396	}
397	
398	// tryExitCodeForStatus maps a bead execution status string to the ddx try exit code contract.
399	func tryExitCodeForStatus(status string) error {
400		switch status {
401		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
402			return nil // exit 0
403		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
404			agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced:
405			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitPreservedOrNone, Message: ""}
406		default:
407			// execution_failed and all other terminal non-success statuses
408			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
409		}
410	}
411	
412	// unmetDeps returns the IDs of deps that are not yet closed, in the order
413	// they appear in the bead's dependency list.
414	func unmetDeps(store *bead.Store, b *bead.Bead) []string {
415		depIDs := b.DepIDs()
416		if len(depIDs) == 0 {
417			return nil
418		}
419		var unmet []string
420		for _, depID := range depIDs {
421			dep, err := store.Get(depID)
422			if err != nil || dep.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
423				unmet = append(unmet, depID)
424			}
425		}
426		return unmet
427	}
428	
429	// singleBeadStore wraps a *bead.Store and overrides ReadyExecution to return
430	// only a single target bead. All other operations are delegated unchanged.
431	// This allows the execute-bead worker to treat the forced bead as if it were
432	// the only entry in the execution-ready queue.
433	type singleBeadStore struct {
434		*bead.Store
435		target bead.Bead
436	}
437	
438	func (s *singleBeadStore) ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error) {
439		return []bead.Bead{s.target}, nil
440	}

16	var validBeadID = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$`)

18	// landingGitOpsFromFactory returns the agent.LandingGitOps the CommandFactory
19	// should use — either the test override or the default RealLandingGitOps.
20	func landingGitOpsFromFactory(f *CommandFactory) agent.LandingGitOps {

24		return agent.RealLandingGitOps{}

27	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
28		cmd := &cobra.Command{
29			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
30			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
31			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
32	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
33	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
34	
35	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
36	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
37	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
38	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
39	
40	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
41	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
42	
43	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx work", which claims ready beads,
44	calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on the result.
45	Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a specific bead.
46	
47	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
48	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
49	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
50	  no_evidence_produced             — agent exited with no commit and no rationale
51	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
52	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
53	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
54	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
55	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
56	
57	ddx work closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
58	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
59	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
60			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "ddx work" for normal queue work)
61	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
62	
63	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
64	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
65	
66	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
67	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
68	
69	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
70	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
71			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
72			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteBead,
73		}
74		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
75		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
76		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-merge", false, "Skip merge; preserve result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<base-shortsha> instead")
77		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
78		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
79		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
80		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
81		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
82		cmd.Flags().String("context-budget", "", "Context budget for prompt: empty (full), minimal (omit large governing docs for cheap-tier)")
83		cmd.Flags().String("prompt", "", "Prompt file path (auto-generated from bead if omitted)")
84		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout (e.g. 60m, 2h); overrides project config and model-class defaults. Use when a thinking model needs more than the default 15 min.")
85		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
86		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
87		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output result as JSON")
88		return cmd
89	}

91	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
92		beadID := args[0]
93		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
94			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
95		}
96		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
97		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
98		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
99		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
100		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
101		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
102		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
103		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
104		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
105		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
106		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
107		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
108		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
109		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
110	
111		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
112	
113		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
114			Harness:       harness,
115			Model:         model,
116			Provider:      provider,
117			ModelRef:      modelRef,
118			Effort:        effort,
119			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
120			MinPower:      minPower,
121			MaxPower:      maxPower,
122		}
123		if requestTimeout > 0 {
124			overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
125		}
126		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
127		if err != nil {
128			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
129		}
130		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
131			FromRev:    fromRev,
132			PromptFile: promptFile,
133			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
134			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
135		}
136	
137		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
138		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
139			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
140		}
141	
142		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
143		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
144			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
145		}
146	
147		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
148		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
149		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
150		// projectRoot internally.
151		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
152			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
153		}
154	
155		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
156		// the queue-work preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`
157		// errors before any git work happens rather than mid-execution. Skip the
158		// preflight when a test runner override is in use — overrides may be
159		// fakes that bypass routing entirely.
160		if f.AgentRunnerOverride == nil {
161			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
162				return err
163			}
164		}
165	
166		// Recover any orphaned worktrees from previous crashed runs before
167		// spawning a new worker. This is the parent's responsibility.
168		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
169	
170		// Worker step: run the agent in an isolated worktree.
171		res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(cmd.Context(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
172		if err != nil && res == nil {
173			return err
174		}
175	
176		// Orchestrator step: land the result (ff or merge → push, gate eval,
177		// preserve). Always run when we have a result — the orchestrator handles
178		// all cases including no-changes and error (agent failed with no commits).
179		//
180		// Single-bead CLI is strictly serial (no sibling workers in this
181		// process), so we can wire the LandingAdvancer to call Land() directly
182		// without going through a per-project coordinator goroutine. Tests may
183		// inject a custom advancer via f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride.
184		if res != nil {
185			var advancer func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error)
186			if f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride != nil {
187				advancer = f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride
188			} else {
189				landingGitOps := landingGitOpsFromFactory(f)
190				advancer = func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
191					return agent.Land(projectRoot, agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), landingGitOps)
192				}
193			}
194			landingOpts := agent.BeadLandingOptions{
195				NoMerge:         noMerge,
196				LandingAdvancer: advancer,
197			}
198	
199			// Wire required execution gates: when the worker manifest declares
200			// governing IDs, gate-eval them in an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev
201			// before LandBeadResult decides merge vs preserve. The original
202			// worker worktree was cleaned up by ExecuteBead, so we pin ResultRev
203			// to a transient ref and check it out into a temp worktree for the
204			// duration of gate evaluation.
205			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
206				wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
207				if ctxErr != nil {
208					// Soft-fail: log and skip gate eval rather than abort the land.
209					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
210				} else if wt != "" {
211					defer cleanup()
212					landingOpts.WtPath = wt
213					landingOpts.GovernIDs = ids
214					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactPath = filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
215					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactRel = filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
216				}
217			}
218	
219			if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchestratorGitOps, landingOpts); landErr == nil {
220				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
221				_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
222			} else if err == nil {
223				err = landErr
224			}
225		}
226	
227		if err != nil {
228			_ = writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
229			return err
230		}
231	
232		return writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
233	}
234	
235	func writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd *cobra.Command, res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult, asJSON bool) error {
236		if res == nil {
237			return nil
238		}
239		if asJSON {
240			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
241			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
242			return enc.Encode(res)
243		}
244	
245		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "bead:    %s\n", res.BeadID)
246		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "base:    %s\n", res.BaseRev)
247		if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
248			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "result:  %s\n", res.ResultRev)
249		}
250		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "outcome: %s\n", res.Outcome)
251		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "status:  %s\n", res.Status)
252		if res.Detail != "" {
253			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "detail:  %s\n", res.Detail)
254		}
255		if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
256			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "rationale: %s\n", res.NoChangesRationale)
257		}
258		if res.PreserveRef != "" {
259			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "ref:     %s\n", res.PreserveRef)
260		}
261		return nil
262	}

130	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the

132	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
133	
134	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
135	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
136	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
137	// triage:needs-investigation park.
138	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
139	
140	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
141	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
142	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
143	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
144	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
145	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
146	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
147	//
148	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
149	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
150	//
151	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
152	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
153	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
154	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
155	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
156	// directly.
157	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
158		ctx context.Context,
159		projectRoot string,
160		b *bead.Bead,
161		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
162		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
163		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
164		actor, source string,
165		now func() time.Time,
166	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
167		if now == nil {
168			now = time.Now
169		}
170		if submit == nil {
171			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
172		}
173		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
174		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
175		if err != nil {
176			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
177			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
178			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
179			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
180			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
181				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
182			}
183			return &LandResult{
184				Status:      "preserved",
185				PreserveRef: ref,
186				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
187			}, nil, nil
188		}
189		if outcome.Blocked {
190			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
191			if perr != nil {
192				return nil, outcome, perr
193			}
194			if eventStore != nil {
195				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
196			}
197			return land, outcome, nil
198		}
199		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
200			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
201		}
202		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
203		return land, outcome, sErr
204	}
205	
206	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
207	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
208	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
209	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.

Desc:     PROBLEM
All 21 ddx agent subcommands remain live in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85 — none are Hidden, none are Deprecated. The migration to ddx run/try/work was supposed to happen but only ddx work was added. Operators still use ddx agent surface, and skill manifests + docs still reference ddx agent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85: 21 cobra command registrations for ddx agent subcommands (run, execute-bead, execute-loop, benchmark, condense, capabilities, replay, list, doctor, log, etc.) are all live. None set Hidden=true or Deprecated.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: execute-loop and execute-bead are aliases; after C13 (ddx-387a0178), ddx work and ddx try replace these.
- .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, skills/: skill manifests reference 'ddx agent run' and 'ddx agent execute-bead'.
- docs/: README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website/content/docs/cli/_index.md reference ddx agent.
- Depends on C13 (ddx-387a0178) which adds ddx try CLI — cannot retire ddx agent until ddx try exists.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Delete cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and all cli/cmd/agent_*.go sibling files.
2. Verify: ddx run + ddx try + ddx work cover every prior ddx agent operation:
   - ddx agent run → ddx run
   - ddx agent execute-loop → ddx work
   - ddx agent execute-bead → ddx try
   - ddx agent list → ddx agent list removed (or migrated to ddx harness list)
   - ddx agent doctor → ddx doctor
   - ddx agent log → ddx log (or removed)
3. Update skill manifests to use new commands.
4. Update docs (README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website CLI docs).

input.prompt:
In the DDx codebase at /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d8474e0e-20260509T231207-8e73f79c, I need to understand:

1. What does `agent.BuildLandingGateContext` return (wtPath, governIDs) and how are these used when passed to `agent.LandBeadResult` via `BeadLandingOptions.WtPath` and `BeadLandingOptions.GovernIDs`? Specifically, read cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go around lines 119-250 to understand what LandBeadResult does with WtPath and GovernIDs.

2. Does `SubmitWithPreMergeChecks` (in execute_bead_loop.go) do any gate evaluation with a worktree, or does it only do the RunPreMergeChecks (checks-based gate)?

3. In `try.go`'s executor closure (around line 276), `SubmitWithPreMergeChecks` is called. What additional gate evaluation (beyond RunPreMergeChecks) would be missed compared to what `agent_execute_bead.go` does with `LandBeadResult` + `BuildLandingGateContext`?

4. What does `agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:390) actually do? Is it a routing check that validates the harness/model combo, or something else? Is it appropriate to call it from `try.go`'s executor, or is it N/A given try.go's passthrough model?

19	// NowFunc allows tests to override time.Now for deterministic PreserveRef output.
20	var NowFunc = time.Now
21	
22	// PreserveRef builds the documented hidden ref for a preserved iteration.
23	func PreserveRef(beadID, baseRev string) string {
24		shortSHA := baseRev
25		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
26			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
27		}
28		timestamp := NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
29		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, timestamp, shortSHA)
30	}

32	// GateCheckResult records the outcome of one required execution gate.
33	type GateCheckResult struct {
34		DefinitionID string `json:"definition_id"`
35		Required     bool   `json:"required"`
36		ExitCode     int    `json:"exit_code"`
37		// Status is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
38		Status string `json:"status"`
39		Stdout string `json:"stdout,omitempty"`
40		Stderr string `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
41		// Ratchet captures the ratchet evaluation when the gate declared
42		// thresholds. Populated for both pass and fail decisions so HELIX can
43		// distinguish ratchet outcomes from generic command failures.
44		Ratchet *RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// RatchetEvidence is the machine-readable record of one ratchet evaluation
48	// performed before landing. Threshold/Observed carry the authored ratchet
49	// and the observed value; Decision is "pass" or "fail"; Reason provides a
50	// short explanation of what was compared ("observed 310 ms > ratchet 250 ms").
51	type RatchetEvidence struct {
52		DefinitionID string  `json:"definition_id"`
53		MetricID     string  `json:"metric_id,omitempty"`
54		Comparison   string  `json:"comparison,omitempty"`
55		Threshold    float64 `json:"threshold"`
56		Observed     float64 `json:"observed"`
57		Unit         string  `json:"unit,omitempty"`
58		Decision     string  `json:"decision"`
59		Reason       string  `json:"reason,omitempty"`
60	}
61	
62	// RatchetPreserveReason marks a landing that was preserved because a declared
63	// ratchet was not met. Callers match on this exact string (via the landing
64	// result's Reason field) to bucket ratchet-preserved attempts apart from
65	// generic gate failures.
66	const RatchetPreserveReason = "ratchet miss"

73		Summary         string            `json:"summary"`

79	// OrchestratorGitOps abstracts the git operations needed by the parent-side
80	// orchestrator for preserving worker results under iteration refs.
81	//
82	// NOTE: The Merge(dir, rev) method that existed here before the land
83	// coordinator redesign has been DELETED. All target-branch writes now flow
84	// through Land() in execute_bead_land.go and its per-project serialized
85	// coordinator. See ddx-8746d8a6 / ddx-e14efc58 / ddx-6aa50e57 for the
86	// rationale: the old path produced "chore: checkpoint before merge" noise
87	// and workers racing on the same projectRoot could corrupt each other's
88	// intermediate state. Land() serializes through a single goroutine and
89	// uses `git merge --no-ff` when the target has advanced, so the worker's
90	// commit is never rewritten and replay sees the same inputs it originally saw.
91	//
92	// The LandingAdvancer field on BeadLandingOptions is the coordinator
93	// injection point for LandBeadResult callers that need to ff the target
94	// branch. When LandingAdvancer is nil (the interactive single-bead CLI),
95	// LandBeadResult falls back to preserving the result under
96	// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... rather than modifying the target branch.
97	type OrchestratorGitOps interface {
98		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
99	}
100	
101	// RealOrchestratorGitOps implements OrchestratorGitOps via os/exec git commands.
102	type RealOrchestratorGitOps struct{}
103	
104	// UpdateRef updates a git ref to point at sha.
105	func (r *RealOrchestratorGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
106		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
107		if err != nil {
108			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
109		}
110		return nil
111	}

113	// BeadLandingOptions controls how the orchestrator lands a completed worker result.
114	type BeadLandingOptions struct {
115		// NoMerge skips the land step and preserves the result under
116		// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... instead.
117		NoMerge bool
118	
119		// WtPath is the path to a worktree checked out at ResultRev, used for gate
120		// evaluation. When empty, gate evaluation is skipped (the worktree has
121		// typically been cleaned up by the time the orchestrator runs).
122		WtPath string
123	
124		// GovernIDs are the governing artifact IDs to use for gate evaluation.
125		// Only used when WtPath is non-empty. Typically extracted from the worker's
126		// manifest artifact via ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest.
127		GovernIDs []string
128	
129		// ChecksArtifactPath is the absolute path to write checks.json. Optional.
130		ChecksArtifactPath string
131		// ChecksArtifactRel is the relative path stored in the result for checks.json.
132		ChecksArtifactRel string
133	
134		// LandingAdvancer, when non-nil, replaces the old in-process Merge step
135		// with the coordinator-pattern Land() call. The callback is expected to
136		// run fetch → (ff or merge) → push serialized against other submissions
137		// for the same projectRoot. When nil, LandBeadResult falls back to
138		// preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/...
139		// rather than touching the target branch.
140		LandingAdvancer func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error)
141	}

143	// BeadLandingResult records the outcome of the orchestrator's landing step.
144	type BeadLandingResult struct {
145		// Outcome is one of: "merged", "preserved", "no-changes".
146		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
147		// Reason is a human-readable explanation of the outcome.
148		Reason string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
149		// PreserveRef is set when the result was preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/...
150		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
151		// GateResults holds the outcome of each required execution gate.
152		GateResults []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
153		// RequiredExecSummary is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
154		RequiredExecSummary string `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
155		// ChecksFile is the relative path to checks.json when gate results were written.
156		ChecksFile string `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
157		// RatchetEvidence aggregates every ratchet-evaluated gate's evidence for
158		// the attempt. Surfaced separately from GateResults so HELIX and other
159		// consumers can tell apart ratchet outcomes from generic gate failures.
160		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
161		// RatchetSummary is "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
162		RatchetSummary string `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
163	}
164	
165	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
166	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
167	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
168	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
169	//
170	// Outcome rules:
171	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
172	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
173	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
174	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
175	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
176	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
177		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
178	
179		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
180		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
181		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
182		agentFailed := res.ExitCode != 0 || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
183		if agentFailed && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
184			landing.Outcome = "error"
185			switch {
186			case res.Reason != "":
187				landing.Reason = res.Reason
188			case res.Error != "":
189				landing.Reason = res.Error
190			default:
191				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
192			}
193			return landing, nil
194		}
195	
196		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
197		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
198			if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence || res.FailureMode == FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced {
199				landing.Outcome = "no-evidence"
200				if res.Reason != "" {
201					landing.Reason = res.Reason
202				} else if res.Error != "" {
203					landing.Reason = res.Error
204				} else {
205					landing.Reason = "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt"
206				}
207				return landing, nil
208			}
209			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
210			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
211				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
212			} else {
213				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
214			}
215			return landing, nil
216		}
217	
218		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
219		if agentFailed {
220			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
221			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
222				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
223			}
224			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
225			landing.PreserveRef = ref
226			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
227			return landing, nil
228		}
229	
230		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
231		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
232		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting
233		// through the coordinator. It mutates res.GateResults / RequiredExecSummary
234		// / RatchetEvidence / RatchetSummary / ChecksFile in place; we mirror the
235		// outputs onto landing so ApplyLandingToResult can copy them back later
236		// without losing the existing landing.* contract.
237		anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gateErr := EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(
238			opts.WtPath, opts.GovernIDs, res, projectRoot,
239			opts.ChecksArtifactPath, opts.ChecksArtifactRel,
240		)
241		if gateErr != nil {
242			return nil, gateErr
243		}
244		gateResults := res.GateResults
245		landing.GateResults = gateResults
246		landing.RequiredExecSummary = res.RequiredExecSummary
247		landing.RatchetEvidence = res.RatchetEvidence
248		landing.RatchetSummary = res.RatchetSummary
249		landing.ChecksFile = res.ChecksFile
250	
251		// Gate failed: preserve instead of merging. Ratchet misses get a
252		// dedicated reason so status/failure_mode classifiers can distinguish
253		// them from generic command failures.
254		if anyGateFailed {
255			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
256			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
257				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
258			}
259			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
260			landing.PreserveRef = ref
261			if anyRatchetFailed {
262				landing.Reason = RatchetPreserveReason
263			} else {
264				landing.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
265			}
266			return landing, nil
267		}
268	
269		// --no-merge: preserve unconditionally.
270		if opts.NoMerge {
271			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
272			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
273				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
274			}
275			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
276			landing.PreserveRef = ref
277			landing.Reason = "--no-merge specified"
278			return landing, nil
279		}
280	
281		// Default: land the worker's commits on the target branch. When a
282		// LandingAdvancer is provided (server coordinator / --local coordinator)
283		// it runs the fetch → (ff or merge) → push sequence serialized per
284		// projectRoot. When no advancer is provided, LandBeadResult falls back
285		// to preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/ — the interactive
286		// single-bead CLI path, which intentionally does NOT auto-advance the
287		// target branch (the operator moves the ref themselves).
288		if opts.LandingAdvancer != nil {
289			land, landErr := opts.LandingAdvancer(res)
290			if landErr != nil {
291				return nil, fmt.Errorf("land advancer: %w", landErr)
292			}
293			switch land.Status {
294			case "landed":
295				landing.Outcome = "merged"
296				if land.Merged {
297					landing.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
298				}
299				if land.NewTip != "" {
300					res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
301				}
302				if land.PushFailed {
303					landing.Reason = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
304				}
305			case "preserved":
306				landing.Outcome = "preserved"
307				landing.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
308				landing.Reason = land.Reason
309			case "no-changes":
310				landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
311				landing.Reason = land.Reason
312			default:
313				landing.Outcome = "preserved"
314				landing.Reason = "unknown land status: " + land.Status
315			}
316			return landing, nil
317		}
318	
319		// No advancer: preserve under refs/ddx/iterations/ as a safe fallback.
320		ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
321		if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
322			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref (no land advancer): %w", err)
323		}
324		landing.Outcome = "preserved"
325		landing.PreserveRef = ref
326		landing.Reason = "no land advancer configured"
327		return landing, nil
328	}
329	
330	// ApplyLandingToResult merges a BeadLandingResult's fields into an
331	// ExecuteBeadResult so callers can output a single unified record. It
332	// overwrites Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults,
333	// RequiredExecSummary, and ChecksFile based on the landing decision.
334	func ApplyLandingToResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, landing *BeadLandingResult) {
335		res.Outcome = landing.Outcome
336		res.Reason = landing.Reason
337		res.PreserveRef = landing.PreserveRef
338		res.GateResults = landing.GateResults
339		res.RequiredExecSummary = landing.RequiredExecSummary
340		res.ChecksFile = landing.ChecksFile
341		res.RatchetEvidence = landing.RatchetEvidence
342		res.RatchetSummary = landing.RatchetSummary
343		// Re-classify status based on landing outcome and reason.
344		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(landing.Outcome, res.ExitCode, landing.Reason)
345		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, landing.Reason, res.Error)
346		// Refine failure_mode with landing-level signals. A clean merge clears
347		// the field; merge conflict or gate failure overrides the worker-level
348		// classification; other preserved outcomes keep the worker's mode.
349		res.FailureMode = classifyLandingFailureMode(landing.Outcome, landing.Reason, landing.GateResults, res.FailureMode)
350		res.OrchestratorStatus = res.Status
351	}
352	
353	// ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest reads governing artifact IDs from a manifest
354	// JSON file at manifestAbs (absolute path). Returns nil when the file cannot
355	// be read. Callers use this to populate BeadLandingOptions.GovernIDs.
356	func ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest(manifestAbs string) []string {
357		if manifestAbs == "" {
358			return nil
359		}
360		type manifestShape struct {
361			Governing []struct {
362				ID string `json:"id"`
363			} `json:"governing"`
364		}
365		raw, err := os.ReadFile(manifestAbs)
366		if err != nil {
367			return nil
368		}
369		var m manifestShape
370		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
371			return nil
372		}
373		ids := make([]string, 0, len(m.Governing))
374		for _, g := range m.Governing {
375			if g.ID != "" {
376				ids = append(ids, g.ID)
377			}
378		}
379		return ids
380	}
381	
382	// RecoverOrphans removes orphaned execute-bead worktrees for a given bead ID.
383	// This is the parent's responsibility — call it before spawning new workers
384	// so stale worktrees from crashed previous attempts do not accumulate.
385	func RecoverOrphans(gitOps GitOps, workDir, beadID string) {
386		paths, err := gitOps.WorktreeList(workDir)
387		if err != nil {
388			return
389		}
390		// Match by basename prefix so orphans are found regardless of their parent
391		// directory (legacy .ddx/ vs new $TMPDIR/ddx-exec-wt/).
392		basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"
393		orphanRemoved := false
394		for _, p := range paths {
395			if strings.HasPrefix(filepath.Base(p), basenamePrefix) {
396				_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, p)
397				orphanRemoved = true
398			}
399		}
400		_ = gitOps.WorktreePrune(workDir)
401	
402		// Sweep stale run-state left by crashed workers. Per-attempt records are
403		// cleared individually so concurrent attempts for other beads remain visible.
404		if states, _ := ReadRunStates(workDir); len(states) > 0 {
405			for _, state := range states {
406				remove := orphanRemoved && state.BeadID == beadID
407				if !remove && state.BeadID == beadID {
408					remove = true
409				}
410				if !remove && state.WorktreePath != "" {
411					if _, err := os.Stat(state.WorktreePath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
412						remove = true
413					}
414				}
415				if remove {
416					_ = ClearRunStateAttempt(workDir, state.AttemptID)
417				}
418			}
419		}
420	}
421	
422	// evaluateRequiredGates resolves graph-authored execution documents that are
423	// required and linked to any of the governing artifact IDs, then runs each
424	// one. When a gate declares a ratchet threshold, the command's numeric output
425	// is compared against the threshold after a successful exit; a ratchet miss
426	// demotes the gate status to "fail" and records the evidence the orchestrator
427	// surfaces as part of its landing decision.
428	//
429	// Returns: gate results (one per candidate), anyFailed (true if any gate's
430	// final status is "fail"), anyRatchetFailed (true if any failure was caused
431	// by a ratchet miss rather than a non-zero exit), and any fatal error from
432	// walking the graph (soft errors are swallowed).
433	func evaluateRequiredGates(wtPath string, governingIDs []string) ([]GateCheckResult, bool, bool, error) {
434		if len(governingIDs) == 0 {
435			return nil, false, false, nil
436		}
437	
438		graph, err := docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig(wtPath)
439		if err != nil {
440			// Soft error: skip gate evaluation rather than blocking all landings.
441			return nil, false, false, nil
442		}
443	
444		governingSet := make(map[string]bool, len(governingIDs))
445		for _, id := range governingIDs {
446			governingSet[id] = true
447		}
448	
449		type execCandidate struct {
450			id         string
451			command    []string
452			cwd        string
453			comparison string
454			thresholds *docgraph.DocThresholds
455			metric     *docgraph.DocMetricSpec
456		}
457		var candidates []execCandidate
458		for _, doc := range graph.Documents {
459			if doc.ExecDef == nil || !doc.ExecDef.Required {
460				continue
461			}
462			ed := doc.ExecDef
463			if ed.Kind != "command" {
464				continue
465			}
466			if len(ed.Command) == 0 {
467				continue
468			}
469			linked := false
470			for _, dep := range doc.DependsOn {
471				if governingSet[dep] {
472					linked = true
473					break
474				}
475			}
476			if !linked {
477				for _, artID := range ed.ArtifactIDs {
478					if governingSet[artID] {
479						linked = true
480						break
481					}
482				}
483			}
484			if !linked {
485				continue
486			}
487			candidates = append(candidates, execCandidate{
488				id:         doc.ID,
489				command:    ed.Command,
490				cwd:        ed.Cwd,
491				comparison: ed.Comparison,
492				thresholds: ed.Thresholds,
493				metric:     ed.Metric,
494			})
495		}
496	
497		if len(candidates) == 0 {
498			return nil, false, false, nil
499		}
500	
501		anyFailed := false
502		anyRatchetFailed := false
503		results := make([]GateCheckResult, 0, len(candidates))
504		for _, c := range candidates {
505			cwd := wtPath
506			if c.cwd != "" {
507				if filepath.IsAbs(c.cwd) {
508					cwd = c.cwd
509				} else {
510					cwd = filepath.Join(wtPath, c.cwd)
511				}
512			}
513	
514			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Minute)
515			cmd := osexec.CommandContext(ctx, c.command[0], c.command[1:]...)
516			cmd.Dir = cwd
517			var stdoutBuf, stderrBuf strings.Builder
518			cmd.Stdout = &stdoutBuf
519			cmd.Stderr = &stderrBuf
520			runErr := cmd.Run()
521			cancel()
522	
523			stdoutStr := strings.TrimSpace(stdoutBuf.String())
524			stderrStr := strings.TrimSpace(stderrBuf.String())
525			gr := GateCheckResult{
526				DefinitionID: c.id,
527				Required:     true,
528				Stdout:       stdoutStr,
529				Stderr:       stderrStr,
530			}
531			if runErr != nil {
532				gr.ExitCode = 1
533				if exitErr, ok := runErr.(*osexec.ExitError); ok {
534					gr.ExitCode = exitErr.ExitCode()
535				}
536				gr.Status = "fail"
537				anyFailed = true
538			} else {
539				gr.Status = "pass"
540			}
541	
542			// Apply ratchet evaluation when thresholds are declared. Only runs
543			// when the command exited cleanly — a non-zero exit already failed
544			// the gate and there's no point parsing unreliable output.
545			if c.thresholds != nil && gr.Status == "pass" {
546				evidence := evaluateRatchet(c.id, c.comparison, c.thresholds, c.metric, stdoutStr, stderrStr)
547				gr.Ratchet = evidence
548				if evidence.Decision == "fail" {
549					gr.Status = "fail"
550					anyFailed = true
551					anyRatchetFailed = true
552				}
553			}
554			results = append(results, gr)
555		}
556	
557		return results, anyFailed, anyRatchetFailed, nil
558	}
559	
560	// evaluateRatchet parses the gate's observed value from stdout and compares it
561	// against the declared ratchet threshold. Always returns a non-nil evidence
562	// record so pass/fail results both carry machine-readable provenance.
563	func evaluateRatchet(definitionID, comparison string, thresholds *docgraph.DocThresholds, metric *docgraph.DocMetricSpec, stdout, stderr string) *RatchetEvidence {
564		if comparison == "" {
565			comparison = "lower-is-better"
566		}
567		unit := ""
568		metricID := ""
569		if metric != nil {
570			unit = metric.Unit
571			metricID = metric.MetricID
572		}
573		if unit == "" {
574			unit = thresholds.Unit
575		}
576		observed, parsedUnit, parsed := parseGateValue(stdout)
577		if !parsed {
578			// Fall back to stderr for tools that report numbers there.
579			observed, parsedUnit, parsed = parseGateValue(stderr)
580		}
581		if unit == "" {
582			unit = parsedUnit
583		}
584		evidence := &RatchetEvidence{
585			DefinitionID: definitionID,
586			MetricID:     metricID,
587			Comparison:   comparison,
588			Threshold:    thresholds.Ratchet,
589			Observed:     observed,
590			Unit:         unit,
591		}
592		if !parsed {
593			evidence.Decision = "fail"
594			evidence.Reason = "gate did not emit a parseable numeric value"
595			return evidence
596		}
597		passed := ratchetPasses(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed)
598		if passed {
599			evidence.Decision = "pass"
600			evidence.Reason = formatRatchetReason(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed, unit, true)
601		} else {
602			evidence.Decision = "fail"
603			evidence.Reason = formatRatchetReason(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed, unit, false)
604		}
605		return evidence
606	}
607	
608	// ratchetPasses returns true when observed satisfies the ratchet constraint.
609	// lower-is-better: observed must be <= threshold. higher-is-better: observed
610	// must be >= threshold. Any other comparison string defaults to lower-is-better.
611	func ratchetPasses(comparison string, threshold, observed float64) bool {
612		if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
613			return observed >= threshold
614		}
615		return observed <= threshold
616	}
617	
618	func formatRatchetReason(comparison string, threshold, observed float64, unit string, passed bool) string {
619		cmpOp := "<="
620		if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
621			cmpOp = ">="
622		}
623		state := "pass"
624		if !passed {
625			state = "fail"
626			// flip operator for failures so the message reads like "observed 310 > ratchet 250"
627			if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
628				cmpOp = "<"
629			} else {
630				cmpOp = ">"
631			}
632		}
633		if unit != "" {
634			return fmt.Sprintf("%s: observed %g %s %s ratchet %g %s", state, observed, unit, cmpOp, threshold, unit)
635		}
636		return fmt.Sprintf("%s: observed %g %s ratchet %g", state, observed, cmpOp, threshold)
637	}
638	
639	// parseGateValue extracts a numeric observation from gate output. Accepts
640	// either a JSON object with a "value" (and optional "unit") field, or a
641	// trailing number with an optional unit suffix. Returns the value, the parsed
642	// unit (may be ""), and whether parsing succeeded.
643	func parseGateValue(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
644		text = strings.TrimSpace(text)
645		if text == "" {
646			return 0, "", false
647		}
648		if v, u, ok := parseGateJSON(text); ok {
649			return v, u, true
650		}
651		return parseGateText(text)
652	}
653	
654	var gateMeasurementPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(?:\s*)([a-zA-Z%/]+)?`)
655	
656	func parseGateJSON(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
657		var obj map[string]any
658		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(text), &obj); err != nil {
659			return 0, "", false
660		}
661		raw, ok := obj["value"]
662		if !ok {
663			return 0, "", false
664		}
665		unit, _ := obj["unit"].(string)
666		switch v := raw.(type) {
667		case float64:
668			return v, unit, true
669		case string:
670			if parsed, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64); err == nil {
671				return parsed, unit, true
672			}
673		}
674		return 0, "", false
675	}
676	
677	func parseGateText(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
678		// Take the last matching measurement so "result: 310 ms" still parses.
679		matches := gateMeasurementPattern.FindAllStringSubmatch(text, -1)
680		if len(matches) == 0 {
681			return 0, "", false
682		}
683		m := matches[len(matches)-1]
684		v, err := strconv.ParseFloat(m[1], 64)
685		if err != nil {
686			return 0, "", false
687		}
688		unit := ""
689		if len(m) >= 3 {
690			unit = strings.TrimSpace(m[2])
691		}
692		return v, unit, true
693	}
694	
695	// collectRatchetEvidence flattens the ratchet evidence from each gate result
696	// into a single slice the orchestrator stores on the landing record.
697	func collectRatchetEvidence(gateResults []GateCheckResult) []RatchetEvidence {
698		var out []RatchetEvidence
699		for _, gr := range gateResults {
700			if gr.Ratchet != nil {
701				out = append(out, *gr.Ratchet)
702			}
703		}
704		return out
705	}
706	
707	// summarizeRatchets returns "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
708	func summarizeRatchets(evidence []RatchetEvidence) string {
709		if len(evidence) == 0 {
710			return ""
711		}
712		for _, e := range evidence {
713			if e.Decision == "fail" {
714				return "fail"
715			}
716		}
717		return "pass"
718	}
719	
720	// summarizeGates returns the RequiredExecSummary string for the landing result.
721	func summarizeGates(results []GateCheckResult, anyFailed bool) string {
722		if len(results) == 0 {
723			return "skipped"
724		}
725		if anyFailed {
726			return "fail"
727		}
728		return "pass"
729	}
