24	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
25	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
26	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
27	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
28	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
29		Log             io.Writer
30		CleanupLog      io.Writer
31		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
32		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
33		CleanupInterval time.Duration
34		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
35		EventSink       io.Writer
36		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
37		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
38		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
39		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
40		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
41		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
42		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
43		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
44		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
45		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
46		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
47		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
48		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
49		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
50		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
51		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
52		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
53		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
54		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
55		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
56		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
57		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
58		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
59		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
60		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
61		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
62		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
63		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
64		Mode         executeloop.Mode
65		IdleInterval time.Duration
66		Once         bool
67		PollInterval time.Duration
68		NoReview     bool
69		LabelFilter  string
70		SessionID    string
71		WorkerID     string
72		ProjectRoot  string
73		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
74		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
75		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
76		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
77		TargetBeadID string
78		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
79		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
80		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
81		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
82		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
83		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
84		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
85	}
86	
87	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
88		mode := r.Mode
89		if mode == "" {
90			if r.Once {
91				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
92			} else {
93				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
94			}
95		}
96		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
97		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
98			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
99		}
100		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
101			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
102		}
103		return mode, idleInterval
104	}
105	
106	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
107	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
108	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
109	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
110	
111	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
112	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
113	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
114	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
115	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
116	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
117	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
118	
119	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
120	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
121	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
122	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
123	// advance the base branch.
124	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
125	
126	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
127	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
128	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
129	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
130	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
131	// worker.
132	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
133	
134	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
135	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
136	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
137	// triage:needs-investigation park.
138	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
139	
140	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
141	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
142	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
143	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
144	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
145	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
146	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
147	//
148	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
149	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
150	//
151	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
152	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
153	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
154	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
155	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
156	// directly.
157	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
158		ctx context.Context,
159		projectRoot string,
160		b *bead.Bead,
161		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
162		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
163		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
164		actor, source string,
165		now func() time.Time,
166	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
167		if now == nil {
168			now = time.Now
169		}
170		if submit == nil {
171			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
172		}
173		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
174		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
175		if err != nil {
176			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
177			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
178			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
179			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
180			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
181				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
182			}
183			return &LandResult{
184				Status:      "preserved",
185				PreserveRef: ref,
186				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
187			}, nil, nil
188		}
189		if outcome.Blocked {
190			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
191			if perr != nil {
192				return nil, outcome, perr
193			}
194			if eventStore != nil {
195				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
196			}
197			return land, outcome, nil
198		}
199		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
200			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
201		}
202		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
203		return land, outcome, sErr
204	}
205	
206	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
207	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
208	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
209	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
210	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
211		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
212		if hook == nil {
213			return report
214		}
215		if isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
216			return report
217		}
218		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
219		if err != nil {
220			if runtime.Log != nil {
221				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
222			}
223			return report
224		}
225		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
226		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
227		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
228		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
229			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
230		}
231		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
232			return report
233		}
234		if classificationRecognized {
235			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
236		}
237		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
238		return report
239	}
240	
241	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
242	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
243	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
244	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
245		if d <= 0 {
246			return 0
247		}
248		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
249			return MaxLoopCooldown
250		}
251		return d
252	}
253	
254	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
255		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
256		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
257		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
258		Harness   string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
259		Provider  string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
260		Model     string `json:"model,omitempty"`
261		// ActualPower is the routing-decision power of the model that actually
262		// served the implementer's call. Forwarded to the post-merge reviewer so
263		// it can request MinPower=actualPower+1 and bias routing toward a
264		// stronger reviewer (R4 pairing).
265		ActualPower                 int           `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
266		PredictedPower              int           `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
267		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64       `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
268		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64       `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
269		PredictedCostSource         string        `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
270		Status                      string        `json:"status"`
271		Detail                      string        `json:"detail,omitempty"`
272		Error                       string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
273		Stderr                      string        `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
274		RateLimitBudget             time.Duration `json:"rate_limit_budget,omitempty"`
275		SessionID                   string        `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
276		BaseRev                     string        `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
277		ResultRev                   string        `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
278		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA before landing.
279		// Mirrors ExecuteBeadResult.ImplementationRev; empty for no-changes attempts.
280		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
281		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator landing.
282		// Distinct from ImplementationRev on the merge-commit path.
283		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
284		// EvidenceRev is the trailing evidence commit SHA when distinct from
285		// ImplementationRev. Empty when not separately committed.
286		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
287		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
288		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
289		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.
290		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
291		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
292		CycleIndex int    `json:"cycle_index,omitempty"`
293		RetryAfter string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
294		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
295		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
296		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
297		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
298		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
299		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
300		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
301		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
302		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
303		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
304		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
305		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
306		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
307		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
308		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
309		// compute wasted/effective spend.
310		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
311		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
312		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
313		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
314		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
315		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
316		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
317		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
318		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
319		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
320		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
321		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
322		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
323		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
324		// recommendation.
325		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
326		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
327		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
328		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
329		// Disrupted marks a failed attempt as worker-disrupted rather than
330		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
331		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
332		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
333		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
334		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
335		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
336		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
337		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
338		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
339		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
340		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
341		// occurring.
342		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
343		// OutcomeReason carries the machine-readable lifecycle classification for
344		// the attempt outcome. It complements Disrupted/DisruptionReason without
345		// changing their mechanical interruption semantics.
346		OutcomeReason string `json:"outcome_reason,omitempty"`
347		// ResourceExhausted carries the execution-root preflight result when the
348		// attempt stopped before the agent could safely continue.
349		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
350	}

474	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
475	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
476	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
477	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
478		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
479		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
480		SatisfactionChecker agenttry.SatisfactionChecker // nil → NoChangesContract default
481		// VerificationRunner runs a verification_command parsed out of a
482		// no_changes_rationale.txt under NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1). When
483		// nil, DefaultVerificationCommandRunner is used.
484		VerificationRunner agenttry.VerificationCommandRunner
485		Now                func() time.Time
486		// Reviewer is retained for legacy/manual review helper tests. Automated
487		// close eligibility is owned by pre-land candidate-cycle review, so the
488		// execute-loop success path no longer calls this post-land reviewer.
489		Reviewer BeadReviewer
490		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is consulted before a bead is claimed.
491		// The zero value fail-opens once and then allows.
492		ComplexityGate work.Guard
493	
494		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
495		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
496		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
497		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
498		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
499		// (escalating to land_conflict_operator_required); false means failed-but-retriable.
500		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)

23	// Exit codes for ddx try terminal outcomes. These are specific to ddx try
24	// and intentionally do not reuse the generic ExitCode* constants because
25	// the bead-execution outcome vocabulary is distinct from generic CLI errors.
26	const (
27		tryExitSuccess         = 0 // success or already_satisfied
28		tryExitPreservedOrNone = 1 // preserved_for_review or no_changes
29		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
30	)
31	
32	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
33	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
34	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
35	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
36	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
37		cmd := &cobra.Command{
38			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
39			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
40			SilenceUsage: true,
41			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
42	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
43	
44	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
45	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
46	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
47	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
48	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
49	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
50	
51	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
52	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
53	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
54	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
55	
56	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
57	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
58	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
59	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
60	
61	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
62	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
63	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
64	
65	Exit codes:
66	  0  success or already_satisfied
67	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
68	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
69	`,
70			Example: `  # Execute a specific bead
71	  ddx try ddx-abc12345
72	
73	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
74	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini
75	
76	  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
77	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing`,
78			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
79			RunE: f.runTry,
80		}
81	
82		// Routing knobs — identical to "ddx work" passthrough surface.
83		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
84		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
85		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
86		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
87		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
89		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
90		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
91		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
92		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
93		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
94		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
95		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
98	
99		return cmd
100	}
101	
102	// runTry is the RunE for "ddx try <bead-id>".
103	func (f *CommandFactory) runTry(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
104		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
105			return err
106		}
107	
108		beadID := args[0]
109	
110		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
111		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
112	
113		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
114		// Runs before claim/worktree setup so the operator sees the warning before
115		// any bead state changes.
116		preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
117		f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
118			emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
119		})
120	
121		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
122		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
123		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
124		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
125		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
126		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
127		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
128		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
129		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
130		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
131		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
132		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
133		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
134		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
135	
136		if noReview && !noReviewAck {
137			return fmt.Errorf("--no-review requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing (break-glass acknowledgement)")
138		}
139	
140		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
141	
142		// Pre-flight: look up the bead.
143		target, err := store.Get(beadID)
144		if err != nil {
145			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead not found: %s\n", beadID)
146			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
147		}
148	
149		// Pre-flight: verify the bead is in a claimable state.
150		// Claimable = open, or stale in_progress (the loop handles stale reclaim).
151		switch target.Status {
152		case bead.StatusOpen, bead.StatusInProgress:
153			// allowed — loop's Claim() handles stale-heartbeat reclaim
154		default:
155			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not claimable (status=%s)\n", target.Status)
156			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
157		}
158	
159		// Pre-flight: check for unmet dependencies.
160		if unmet := unmetDeps(store, target); len(unmet) > 0 {
161			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead has unmet dependencies: %s\n", strings.Join(unmet, ", "))
162			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
163		}
164	
165		// Wrap the real store so that ReadyExecution() returns only the target bead.
166		// All other operations pass through to the underlying store unchanged.
167		forcedStore := &singleBeadStore{Store: store, target: *target}
168	
169		// Set up progress logging (same as runAgentExecuteLoopImpl).
170		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
171		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
172		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
173		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
174		var loopSink io.Writer
175		if lf, openErr := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); openErr == nil {
176			loopSink = lf
177			defer lf.Close() //nolint:errcheck
178		}
179	
180		// Worker-probe (best-effort server mirror; non-blocking).
181		probeIdent := workerprobe.Identity{
182			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
183			Harness:      harness,
184			Model:        model,
185			ExecutorPID:  os.Getpid(),
186			ExecutorHost: hostnameOrEmpty(),
187			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
188		}
189		probe := workerprobe.New(probeIdent, workerprobe.Config{
190			AddrFunc: serverpkg.ReadServerAddr,
191		})
192		probeCtx, probeCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
193		probe.Start(probeCtx)
194		defer func() {
195			probeCancel()
196			probe.Stop()
197		}()
198		if loopSink != nil {
199			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(loopSink, probe)
200		} else {
201			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(io.Discard, probe)
202		}
203	
204		// Process-local LandCoordinator.
205		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
206		defer localCoord.Stop()
207	
208		// Post-merge reviewer (on by default, skipped via --no-review).
209		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
210		if !noReview {
211			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
212				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
213				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
214				BeadEvents:  bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
215				Harness:     reviewHarness,
216				Model:       reviewModel,
217			}
218		}
219	
220		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
221	
222		// Determine whether zero-config auto-route applies (same logic as runWork).
223		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
224			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
225		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
226		resourceChecker := buildCLIResourceChecker(projectRoot, f.resourceCheckerOverride)
227	
228		// Build the executor: either the test override or the real single-tier executor.
229		var executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
230		if f.tryExecutorOverride != nil {
231			executor = f.tryExecutorOverride
232		} else {
233			executor = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, execBeadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
234				var inferredTier escalation.ModelTier
235				if autoInferTier {
236					if b, getErr := store.Get(execBeadID); getErr == nil {
237						inferredTier = escalation.InferTier(b)
238					} else {
239						inferredTier = escalation.TierCheap
240					}
241				}
242	
243				loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{
244					Harness:           harness,
245					Model:             model,
246					Provider:          provider,
247					ModelRef:          modelRef,
248					Profile:           profile,
249					Effort:            effort,
250					MinPower:          minPower,
251					MaxPower:          maxPower,
252					OpaquePassthrough: true, // ddx try treats flags as opaque passthrough
253				}
254				if requestTimeout > 0 {
255					loopOverrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
256				}
257				attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, loopOverrides)
258				gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
259				res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, execBeadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
260					FromRev:         fromRev,
261					Output:          cmd.OutOrStdout(),
262					BeadEvents:      bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
263					ResourceChecker: resourceChecker,
264				}, gitOps)
265				if execErr != nil && res == nil {
266					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
267				}
268				if res != nil && agent.IsResourceExhaustedStatus(res.Status) {
269					return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
270				}
271				if execErr != nil {
272					agent.MarkResultExecutionError(res, execErr)
273					return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
274				}
275				if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
276					targetBead, _ := store.Get(execBeadID)
277					landRes, _, landErr := agent.SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
278						ctx, projectRoot, targetBead, res,
279						func(req agent.LandRequest) (*agent.LandResult, error) { return localCoord.Submit(req) },
280						bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
281						resolveClaimAssignee(), "ddx try",
282						nil,
283					)
284					if landErr == nil {
285						agent.ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res, landRes)
286						_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
287					} else {
288						agent.MarkResultLandError(projectRoot, res, landErr)
289						_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
290						return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
291					}
292				} else if res != nil && (res.Outcome == agent.ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed || res.ExitCode != 0) {
293					if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
294						res.Outcome = "preserved"
295					} else {
296						res.Outcome = "error"
297					}
298					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
299				} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
300					res.Outcome = "no-changes"
301					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
302				}
303				tierStr := ""
304				if inferredTier != "" {
305					tierStr = string(inferredTier)
306				}
307				return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, tierStr), nil
308			})
309		}
310	
311		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
312			Store:    forcedStore,
313			Executor: executor,
314			Reviewer: reviewer,
315		}

319			Harness:  harness,
320			Model:    model,
321			Provider: provider,
322			ModelRef: modelRef,
323			Profile:  profile,
324			Effort:   effort,
325		}
326		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
327		if err != nil {
328			return fmt.Errorf("load resolved config: %w", err)
329		}
330		if _, err := resourceChecker.Check(cmd.Context()); err != nil {
331			if resErr, ok := err.(*agent.ResourceExhaustedError); ok && resErr != nil {
332				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), resErr.Error())
333			}
334			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: err.Error()}
335		}
336	
337		cliLandingOps := agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
338		var qualityRunner agent.AgentRunner
339		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
340			qualityRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
341		}
342		lintHook := agent.NewPreDispatchLintHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner)
343		triageHook := agent.NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner, nil)
344		result, runErr := worker.Run(cmd.Context(), rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
345			Mode:                  executeloop.ModeOnce,
346			Log:                   cmd.OutOrStdout(),
347			EventSink:             loopSink,
348			WorkerID:              resolveClaimAssignee(),
349			ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,

351			SessionID:             loopSessionID,
352			PreClaimHook:          buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps),
353			PreDispatchLintHook:   lintHook,
354			PostAttemptTriageHook: triageHook,
355			NoReview:              noReview,
356		})
357		if runErr != nil {
358			if (errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(runErr, context.DeadlineExceeded)) && result != nil && len(result.Results) > 0 {
359				writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.Results[0])
360			}
361			return runErr
362		}
363	
364		// Report outcome.
365		if result.NoReadyWork {
366			// Bead was not in the execution-ready queue even after pre-flight passed.
367			// This can happen if a heartbeat re-appeared just before claim, making it
368			// not stale-enough for reclaim. Surface a clear message.
369			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not execution-ready (it may be actively claimed by another worker): %s\n", beadID)
370			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
371		}
372	
373		if len(result.Results) == 0 {
374			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
375		}
376		report := result.Results[0]
377		writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), report)
378		if intent := escalation.ParseExecutionHint(target); intent.Source == escalation.ExecutionIntentSourceBeadHint && intent.RequestedTier != "" {
379			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "routing intent: tier=%s source=%s\n", intent.RequestedTier, intent.Source)
380		}
381	
382		return tryExitCodeForStatus(report.Status)
383	}
384	
385	func writeTryResult(w io.Writer, report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
386		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nbead: %s\nstatus: %s\n", report.BeadID, report.Status)
387		if report.Detail != "" {
388			fmt.Fprintf(w, "detail: %s\n", report.Detail)
389		}
390		if report.ResultRev != "" {
391			fmt.Fprintf(w, "result_rev: %s\n", report.ResultRev)
392		}
393		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
394			fmt.Fprintf(w, "preserve_ref: %s\n", report.PreserveRef)
395		}
396	}

7	// reviewRepairAttemptArgs bundles the arguments that must be forwarded to
8	// singleTierAttempt for a review_fixable_gap repair cycle. MinPower is raised
9	// above the implementer's actual power; all other routing pins are forwarded
10	// unchanged from the original attempt.
11	type reviewRepairAttemptArgs struct {
12		MinPower int
13		Harness  string

21	// reviewFixableGapRepairMinPower returns the MinPower floor for a repair
22	// attempt that follows a review_fixable_gap verdict. It advances the
23	// escalation ladder from the implementer's actual power to the next higher
24	// floor so the repair runs on a stronger model than the original implementer.
25	//
26	// If the ladder has no higher floor (exhausted), it returns implActualPower
27	// so the repair attempt at least carries the review rationale as additional
28	// context even if the same power tier is used.
29	func reviewFixableGapRepairMinPower(repairCtx *agent.RepairContextFromReviewGroup, ladder escalationFloorFinder) int {
30		if repairCtx == nil {
31			return 0
32		}
33		next, err := nextEscalationFloor(ladder, repairCtx.ImplementerActualPower)
34		if err != nil {
35			return repairCtx.ImplementerActualPower
36		}
37		return next
38	}
39	
40	// reviewFixableGapRepairArgs computes the singleTierAttempt arguments for a
41	// repair attempt. MinPower is raised above the implementer's actual power
42	// using the escalation ladder. All explicit harness/model/provider/profile
43	// pins are forwarded unchanged. MaxPower is preserved from the operator's
44	// original setting (0 means unset — the repair must not add a cap the
45	// operator did not request).
46	func reviewFixableGapRepairArgs(

48		harness, model, provider, profile string,
49		maxPower int,
50		ladder escalationFloorFinder,
51	) reviewRepairAttemptArgs {
52		return reviewRepairAttemptArgs{
53			MinPower: reviewFixableGapRepairMinPower(repairCtx, ladder),
54			Harness:  harness,
55			Model:    model,
56			Provider: provider,

10	// BuildLandingGateContext prepares the inputs the gate evaluator needs to
11	// score a worker's result before LandBeadResult decides merge-vs-preserve:
12	//
13	//  1. Pin res.ResultRev to refs/ddx/gate-pins/<beadID>/<attemptID>
14	//     so the SHA stays alive while the ephemeral worktree exists.
15	//  2. WorktreeAdd a temp worktree (under os.TempDir() with prefix
16	//     "ddx-gate-wt-") at the pinned ref so gate evaluators can read the
17	//     result tree without disturbing the main worktree.
18	//  3. Read the worker manifest from that result tree and extract governing IDs.
19	//     When the manifest declares no governing IDs the temporary worktree is
20	//     removed immediately and the caller skips gate evaluation.
21	//
22	// The returned cleanup closure removes the worktree and deletes the pin ref.
23	// Callers MUST defer cleanup() before returning to avoid orphaned worktrees.
24	//
25	// On any error the function attempts to roll back partial state (pin+remove)
26	// and returns ("", nil, noop, err).
27	func BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps GitOps) (wtPath string, governIDs []string, cleanup func(), err error) {
28		noop := func() {}
29	
30		if res == nil || res.ManifestFile == "" {
31			return "", nil, noop, nil
32		}
33		if res.ResultRev == "" {
34			return "", nil, noop, fmt.Errorf("BuildLandingGateContext: ResultRev empty, cannot pin")
35		}
36	
37		// Pin ResultRev so the worktree-add target stays alive even though the worker
38		// worktree was removed. Use bead+attempt to avoid collisions across attempts.
39		attemptKey := res.AttemptID
40		if attemptKey == "" {
41			attemptKey = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
42		}
43		pinRef := fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/gate-pins/%s/%s", res.BeadID, attemptKey)
44		if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, pinRef, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
45			return "", nil, noop, fmt.Errorf("pin ResultRev: %w", upErr)
46		}
47		unpin := func() { _ = gitOps.DeleteRef(projectRoot, pinRef) }
48	
49		wt, mkErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-gate-wt-"+attemptKey+"-")
50		if mkErr != nil {
51			unpin()
52			return "", nil, noop, fmt.Errorf("create temp worktree dir: %w", mkErr)
53		}
54		// os.MkdirTemp creates the directory, but `git worktree add` refuses to
55		// run when the target already exists. Remove it so git can recreate it.
56		_ = os.RemoveAll(wt)
57		if addErr := gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wt, res.ResultRev); addErr != nil {
58			_ = os.RemoveAll(wt)
59			unpin()
60			return "", nil, noop, fmt.Errorf("worktree add at %s: %w", res.ResultRev, addErr)
61		}
62	
63		cleanup = func() {
64			_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(projectRoot, wt)
65			_ = os.RemoveAll(wt) // belt-and-suspenders if WorktreeRemove failed
66			unpin()
67		}
68	
69		manifestPath := filepath.Join(wt, filepath.FromSlash(res.ManifestFile))
70		if _, statErr := os.Stat(manifestPath); statErr != nil {
71			manifestPath = filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(res.ManifestFile))
72		}
73		if _, statErr := os.Stat(manifestPath); statErr != nil {
74			cleanup()
75			return "", nil, noop, nil
76		}
77		ids := ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest(manifestPath)
78		if len(ids) == 0 {
79			cleanup()
80			return "", nil, noop, nil
81		}
82		return wt, ids, cleanup, nil
83	}
84	
85	// EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical "run gate evaluation and
86	// write the bundle artifacts" sequence used by LandBeadResult and by upstream
87	// callers (server execute-loop) that want gate eval before submitting through
88	// the coordinator. It delegates to evaluateRequiredGates for the actual gate
89	// scoring, then mutates res to set GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
90	// RatchetEvidence, and RatchetSummary, and writes checks.json into the
91	// execution bundle. Returns (anyRequiredFailed, ratchetFailed, err).
92	//
93	// When wtPath is empty or no governing IDs are provided, gate evaluation is
94	// skipped and res.RequiredExecSummary is set to "skipped" (matching
95	// summarizeGates behavior). checksArtifactPath, when non-empty and gate
96	// results exist, is the absolute path checks.json is written to;
97	// checksArtifactRel is the bundle-relative path stored in res.ChecksFile on
98	// successful write.
99	func EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(wtPath string, governIDs []string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, projectRoot string, checksArtifactPath, checksArtifactRel string) (anyRequiredFailed bool, ratchetFailed bool, err error) {
100		if res == nil {
101			return false, false, fmt.Errorf("EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult: res is nil")
102		}
103	
104		var gateResults []GateCheckResult
105		var anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed bool
106		if wtPath != "" && len(governIDs) > 0 {
107			var gerr error
108			gateResults, anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gerr = evaluateRequiredGates(wtPath, governIDs)
109			if gerr != nil {
110				return false, false, fmt.Errorf("evaluating required gates: %w", gerr)
111			}
112		}
113	
114		res.GateResults = gateResults
115		res.RequiredExecSummary = summarizeGates(gateResults, anyGateFailed)
116		res.RatchetEvidence = collectRatchetEvidence(gateResults)
117		res.RatchetSummary = summarizeRatchets(res.RatchetEvidence)
118	
119		// Write checks.json when gate evaluation produced results and a path was
120		// provided. Best-effort: a write failure leaves res.ChecksFile empty but
121		// does not abort the gate evaluation.
122		if len(gateResults) > 0 && checksArtifactPath != "" {
123			checks := executeBeadChecks{
124				AttemptID:       res.AttemptID,
125				EvaluatedAt:     time.Now().UTC(),
126				Summary:         res.RequiredExecSummary,
127				Results:         gateResults,
128				RatchetSummary:  res.RatchetSummary,
129				RatchetEvidence: res.RatchetEvidence,
130			}
131			if writeErr := writeArtifactJSON(checksArtifactPath, checks); writeErr == nil {
132				res.ChecksFile = checksArtifactRel
133			}
134		}
135	
136		return anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, nil
137	}

26	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
27	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
28	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
29	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31	// returned to a caller.
32	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
33		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
34		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
35		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
36		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
37		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
38		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA — the candidate revision
39		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
40		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
41		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
42		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
43		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
46		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
47		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
48		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
49	
50		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
51		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
52		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
53		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
54		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
55		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
56		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
57		// OrchestratorStatus is populated after the landing/pre-merge orchestration
58		// decision so result.json can distinguish worker success from final
59		// non-landing outcomes.
60		OrchestratorStatus string `json:"orchestrator_status,omitempty"`
61	
62		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
63		Reason      string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
64		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
65		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
66		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.
67		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
68		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
69		CycleIndex          int               `json:"cycle_index,omitempty"`
70		GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
71		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
72		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
73		// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
74		// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
75		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
76		// generic execution failures.
77		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
78		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
79	
80		// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
81		// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
82		// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
83		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
84	
85		// NoEvidencePaths names worktree paths that remained dirty when the agent
86		// exited without creating a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt. It helps
87		// operators diagnose silent commit failures before the worktree is cleaned up.
88		NoEvidencePaths []string `json:"no_evidence_paths,omitempty"`
89	
90		// ResourceExhausted captures the root and cleanup summary when execution
91		// stopped before a bead attempt because the host could not safely continue.
92		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
93	
94		Harness                     string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
95		Provider                    string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
96		Model                       string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
97		ActualPower                 int     `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
98		PredictedPower              int     `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
99		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64 `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
100		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64 `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
101		PredictedCostSource         string  `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
102		SessionID                   string  `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
103		DurationMS                  int     `json:"duration_ms"`
104		Tokens                      int     `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
105		CostUSD                     float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
106		ExitCode                    int     `json:"exit_code"`
107		Error                       string  `json:"error,omitempty"`
108	
109		// FailureMode classifies why an execution did not land cleanly. Empty
110		// when the bead was merged (task_succeeded landing outcome). Populated
111		// by the orchestrator from known patterns; see ClassifyFailureMode and
112		// the FailureMode* constants in execute_bead_status.go.
113		FailureMode string `json:"failure_mode,omitempty"`
114	
115		ExecutionDir       string             `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
116		PromptFile         string             `json:"prompt_file,omitempty"`
117		ManifestFile       string             `json:"manifest_file,omitempty"`
118		ResultFile         string             `json:"result_file,omitempty"`
119		UsageFile          string             `json:"usage_file,omitempty"`
120		WorktreePath       string             `json:"worktree_path,omitempty"`
121		AttemptDiagnostics *AttemptDiagnostic `json:"attempt_diagnostics,omitempty"`
122		Stderr             string             `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
123		RateLimitBudget    time.Duration      `json:"rate_limit_budget,omitempty"`
124	
125		StartedAt  time.Time `json:"started_at"`
126		FinishedAt time.Time `json:"finished_at"`
127	}

129	// AttemptDiagnostic captures infrastructure state when an attempt cannot be
130	// inspected normally. It is intentionally small and JSON-native so result.json
131	// remains useful even when the worker worktree has already vanished.
132	type AttemptDiagnostic struct {

138		CleanupMetadataPresent bool      `json:"cleanup_metadata_present"`
139		RunStatePresent        bool      `json:"run_state_present"`
140		RunState               *RunState `json:"run_state,omitempty"`
141		GitWorktreeRegistered  bool      `json:"git_worktree_registered"`
142	}

## Analysis Report: DDx Agent Module Integration and Export Dependencies

Based on comprehensive code analysis, here are the answers to your questions:

### 1. BuildLandingGateContext Return Values and LandBeadResult Usage

`BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)` returns:
- **wtPath** (string): Path to a temporary worktree checked out at ResultRev under `os.TempDir()` with prefix "ddx-gate-wt-"
- **governIDs** ([]string): Governing artifact IDs extracted from the worker's manifest file
- **cleanup** (func()): Cleanup closure that removes the worktree and deletes the transient pin ref (refs/ddx/gate-pins/<beadID>/<attemptID>)
- **err**: Error if pinning/worktree-add failed

When passed to `LandBeadResult` via `BeadLandingOptions`:
- **WtPath** is passed to `EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult()` along with **GovernIDs** (line 237-239 of execute_bead_orchestrator.go)
- `EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult()` calls `evaluateRequiredGates(wtPath, governIDs)` which:
  - Loads the docgraph from wtPath
  - Filters for required execution documents (kind="command") linked to any governing ID
  - Runs each as a shell command with a 5-minute timeout
  - Evaluates ratchet thresholds when declared
  - Returns GateCheckResult array which is mirrored to landing.GateResults
- If any gate fails or ratchet is missed, landing outcome becomes "preserved" instead of "merged"
- Gate results are written to checks.json at ChecksArtifactPath and ChecksFile is set on the result

### 2. SubmitWithPreMergeChecks Gate Evaluation Scope

`SubmitWithPreMergeChecks()` (execute_bead_loop.go:157-204) performs **ONLY checks-based gate evaluation**:
- Calls `RunPreMergeChecks()` which evaluates `.ddx/checks/*.yaml` gates against (baseRev, resultRev)
- Does NOT evaluate required execution gates (docgraph required documents)
- Does NOT use BuildLandingGateContext or worktree context
- When checks block, preserves result under refs/ddx/iterations/ without attempting merge
- Records checks-bypass events when bypasses are honored

This is fundamentally different from `agent_execute_bead.go`'s `LandBeadResult()` path, which adds required-execution-gate evaluation after checks pass.

### 3. Missing Gate Evaluation in try.go's SubmitWithPreMergeChecks Path

The try.go executor (lines 275-282) calls `SubmitWithPreMergeChecks()` directly without calling `LandBeadResult()`. This means:
- **Missing**: Worktree-based required execution gate evaluation (docgraph checks)
- **Missing**: Ratchet threshold evaluation and evidence collection
- **Missing**: Post-gate decision to preserve vs merge based on gate results
- **Intact**: Pre-merge checks evaluation (.ddx/checks/*.yaml gates) — this still runs

The worktree from try.go's ExecuteBead has been cleaned up by the time SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is called, so even if gate eval were added, try.go would need to rebuild it via BuildLandingGateContext (same as agent_execute_bead.go does).

### 4. ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService Purpose and Appropriateness

`ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(ctx, workDir, harnessName, model, provider, modelRef)` (service_run.go:452-499):
- **Routing validation**, not harness/model compatibility check
- When harness is empty: no-op (routing picks at claim time)
- When harness is set: confirms harness exists in service registry via `ListHarnesses()`
- When model is also set, provider and modelRef are empty, and harness=="agent": calls `ResolveRoute()` preflight to catch orphaned models early

**Appropriateness for try.go**: Yes, appropriate and already in use. agent_execute_bead.go calls it at line 161 as a preflight before any worktree work. try.go's executor does NOT call it (line 259's ExecuteBeadWithConfig wraps the call via config resolution but doesn't directly invoke the validation). For try.go to align with agent_execute_bead.go, this preflight should be added before the try executor's main invocation, but it's a routing preflight, not a gate-evaluation concern.

### 5. agent_execute_loop_escalation.go and agent_execute_loop_review_repair.go Exports

**agent_execute_loop_escalation.go**: All functions are **unexported** (lowercase):
- `isBudgetExhaustedFailure()`, `nextEscalationFloor()`, `highestViableEscalationFloor()`
- `investigationRetryInitialMinPower()`, `triageTierHint()`, `smartRouteUnavailableReport()`
- `runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts()`
- These are used only within cmd/agent_execute_loop.go

**agent_execute_loop_review_repair.go**: All functions are **unexported** (lowercase):
- `reviewFixableGapRepairMinPower()`, `reviewFixableGapRepairArgs()`
- Used only within cmd/agent_execute_loop.go

**Verdict**: Neither file exports anything visible outside cmd/ package. Both are internal helpers for the execute-loop command. Safe to delete or reorganize without breaking external callers.

### 6. Agent Module Exported Functions/Types Referenced from Outside agent_*.go Files

**Critical exports referenced from cmd/ (commands) and internal/server/ (workers.go)**:

| Function/Type | Used By | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `BuildLandingGateContext` | cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:206, server/workers.go:? | Gate context setup before orchestration |
| `RecoverOrphans` | cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:168 | Orphan worktree cleanup before worker spawn |
| `LandBeadResult` | cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:219 | Orchestrator landing decision |
| `ApplyLandingToResult` | cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:220 | Merge landing into result for output |
| `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` | cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:171, try.go:259 | Worker execution |
| `SubmitWithPreMergeChecks` | execute_bead_loop.go:177, try.go:277 | Checks + land sequence |
| `ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` | cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:161 | Routing preflight |
| `EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult` | landing_gate_context.go (canonical), called by LandBeadResult | Gate scoring |
| `ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest` | landing_gate_context.go:77, orchestrator.go | Manifest parsing |
| `PreserveRef` | orchestrator.go | Iteration ref generation |
| `ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult` | try.go:285, execute_bead_loop.go | Merge land result into execution result |

**When agent_cmd.go is deleted** (if agent_execute_loop_escalation and agent_execute_loop_review_repair are moved):
- No breakage — those files only contain unexported helpers
- Deletion would require moving escalation/repair logic into agent/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go or a new agent/escalation/attempt.go file
- The cmd/agent_* exported functions remain intact and continue to be called from cmd/agent_execute_bead.go and cmd/try.go

**Recommendations for lifting into try.go**:
1. `BuildLandingGateContext` and `RecoverOrphans` are safe to lift — they're thin orchestration wrappers with no execute-loop-specific logic
2. Both have all dependencies (GitOps interface, ExecuteBeadResult) available in cmd/ already
3. The gate-evaluation path (EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult → evaluateRequiredGates) can remain in agent/ package; try.go would call it the same way agent_execute_bead.go does
4. Suggested landing: keep BuildLandingGateContext/RecoverOrphans in agent/ package; try.go calls them via existing `agent.*` imports

150		rootCmd.SetVersionTemplate(versionTemplate)
151	
152		// Setup flags - these are now local to this command instance
153		rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&cfgFile, "config", "", "config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)")
154		rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVarP(&verbose, "verbose", "v", false, "verbose output")
155		rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&libraryPath, "library-base-path", "", "override path for DDx library location")
156	
157		// Store flag values in command context for access by subcommands
158		rootCmd.PersistentPreRunE = func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
159			// Initialize config with the local viper instance
160			f.initConfig(cfgFile, libraryPath)
161	
162			// Version gate: block old binary from running in newer project
163			if err := f.checkVersionGate(cmd); err != nil {
164				return err
165			}
166	
167			// Check for updates (synchronous, once per 24h)
168			f.checkForUpdates(cmd)
169	
170			// Call the original PersistentPreRun if it exists
171			if rootCmd.PersistentPreRun != nil {
172				rootCmd.PersistentPreRun(cmd, args)
173			}
174			return nil
175		}
176	
177		// Display update notification and staleness hints after command completes
178		rootCmd.PersistentPostRunE = func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
179			if err := f.displayUpdateNotification(cmd); err != nil {
180				return err
181			}
182			f.displayStalenessHints(cmd)
183			return nil
184		}
185	
186		// Add all subcommands
187		f.registerSubcommands(rootCmd)
188	
189		// Keep the persona APIs in the static production graph. Cobra dispatch
190		// does not always show up in deadcode RTA, so this explicit hook keeps the
191		// real CLI implementations visible without changing runtime behavior.
192		persona.KeepReachabilityForDeadcode()
193		metaprompt.KeepReachabilityForDeadcode()
194		metric.KeepReachabilityForDeadcode()
195	
196		return rootCmd
197	}
198	
199	func formattedVersion(version string) string {
200		if version == "dev" {
201			return "v0.0.1-dev"
202		}
203		if !strings.HasPrefix(version, "v") {
204			return "v" + version
205		}
206		return version
207	}
208	
209	func (f *CommandFactory) versionOutput() string {
210		return fmt.Sprintf("DDx %s\nCommit: %s\nBuilt: %s\n", formattedVersion(f.Version), f.Commit, f.Date)
211	}
212	
213	// initConfig initializes configuration for this command instance
214	func (f *CommandFactory) initConfig(cfgFile, libPath string) {
215		// Store library path override if provided
216		if libPath != "" {
217			_ = os.Setenv("DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH", libPath)
218		}
219	
220		if cfgFile != "" {
221			// Use config file from the flag
222			f.viperInstance.SetConfigFile(cfgFile)
223		} else {
224			// Find home directory
225			home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
226			if err == nil {
227				// Search for config in home directory with name ".ddx" (without extension)
228				f.viperInstance.AddConfigPath(home)
229				f.viperInstance.AddConfigPath(".")
230				f.viperInstance.SetConfigType("yaml")
231				f.viperInstance.SetConfigName(".ddx")
232			}
233		}
234	
235		f.viperInstance.AutomaticEnv() // read in environment variables that match
236	
237		// If a config file is found, read it in
238		if err := f.viperInstance.ReadInConfig(); err == nil {
239			if verbose := f.viperInstance.GetBool("verbose"); verbose {
240				_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Using config file:", f.viperInstance.ConfigFileUsed())
241			}
242		}
243	}
244	
245	// checkForUpdates performs automatic update check (synchronous, once per 24h)
246	func (f *CommandFactory) checkForUpdates(cmd *cobra.Command) {
247		// Check if disabled via env var
248		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
249			return
250		}
251	
252		// Load config
253		cfg, err := config.Load()
254		if err != nil {
255			// Silent failure - use defaults
256			cfg = config.DefaultNewConfig()
257		}
258	
259		// Check if disabled via config
260		if cfg.UpdateCheck != nil && !cfg.UpdateCheck.Enabled {
261			return
262		}
263	
264		// Create checker and perform check (synchronous)
265		checker := update.NewChecker(f.Version, cfg)
266		f.updateMu.Lock()
267		f.updateChecker = checker
268		f.updateDone = make(chan struct{})
269		done := f.updateDone
270		f.updateMu.Unlock()
271	
272		// Run the check asynchronously so command startup is never blocked.
273		go func() {
274			defer close(done)
275			_, _ = checker.CheckForUpdate(context.Background())
276			// Also refresh plugin version cache while we have a network call in flight.
277			f.refreshPluginVersionCache()
278		}()
279	}
280	
281	// displayUpdateNotification shows update notification if available
282	func (f *CommandFactory) displayUpdateNotification(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
283		// Skip if disabled via environment variable
284		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
285			return nil
286		}
287	
288		f.updateMu.Lock()
289		checker := f.updateChecker
290		done := f.updateDone
291		f.updateMu.Unlock()
292	
293		if checker == nil {
294			return nil
295		}
296	
297		if done != nil {
298			select {
299			case <-done:
300			default:
301				return nil
302			}
303		}
304	
305		// Skip update notification for help commands
306		if cmd.Name() == "help" || cmd.Parent() != nil && cmd.Parent().Name() == "help" {
307			return nil
308		}
309	
310		// Skip update notification for machine-readable output formats
311		jsonFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
312		if jsonFlag {
313			return nil
314		}
315	
316		// Skip update notification if silent flag is set
317		silentFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("silent")
318		if silentFlag {
319			return nil
320		}
321	
322		// Skip update notification if --no-check flag is set (for version command)
323		noCheck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-check")
324		if noCheck {
325			return nil
326		}
327	
328		// Skip when stdout is not a terminal (pipes, scripts, CI)
329		if !isatty.IsTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) && !isatty.IsCygwinTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) {
330			return nil
331		}
332	
333		available, version, err := checker.IsUpdateAvailable()
334		if err != nil || !available {
335			if err != nil {
336				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: Could not check for updates: %v\n", err)
337			}
338			return nil
339		}
340	
341		// Show update notification with changelog for version command
342		// Always write to stderr so it doesn't corrupt JSON pipelines
343		isVersionCmd := cmd.Use == "version"
344		if isVersionCmd {
345			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
346				"\n⬆️  Update available: %s (run 'ddx upgrade' to install)\n\nWhat's new:\n  • Performance improvements\n  • Bug fixes\n  • New features\n",
347				version)
348		} else {
349			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
350				"\n⬆️  Update available: %s (run 'ddx upgrade' to install)\n",
351				version)
352		}
353	
354		return nil
355	}
356	
357	// checkVersionGate blocks execution if the binary is older than the project requires.
358	// Returns an error for non-exempt commands when binary version < project's ddx_version.
359	func (f *CommandFactory) checkVersionGate(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
360		// Dev builds bypass the gate
361		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
362			return nil
363		}
364	
365		// Read project versions
366		projectVersion := readProjectVersions(f.WorkingDir)
367		if projectVersion == "" || projectVersion == "dev" {
368			return nil // No versions.yaml or dev project — skip
369		}
370	
371		// Exempt commands that must work even when binary is too old
372		switch cmd.Name() {
373		case "upgrade", "version", "doctor", "init", "help", "completion":
374			return nil
375		}
376	
377		// Compare: is the binary older than what the project requires?
378		needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(f.Version, projectVersion)
379		if err != nil {
380			return nil // Parse error — don't block
381		}
382		if needsUpgrade {
383			return fmt.Errorf("this project requires DDx v%s or newer (you have %s).\nRun 'ddx upgrade' to update your DDx binary",
384				strings.TrimPrefix(projectVersion, "v"),
385				f.Version)
386		}
387	
388		return nil
389	}
390	
391	// displayStalenessHints shows soft hints when project skills or plugins are outdated.
392	func (f *CommandFactory) displayStalenessHints(cmd *cobra.Command) {
393		// Skip for machine-readable output
394		if jsonFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json"); jsonFlag {
395			return
396		}
397		if silentFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("silent"); silentFlag {
398			return
399		}
400		// Skip when stdout is not a terminal (pipes, scripts, CI)
401		if !isatty.IsTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) && !isatty.IsCygwinTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) {
402			return
403		}
404	
405		// Dev builds don't hint
406		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
407			return
408		}
409	
410		// Check project staleness
411		projectVersion := readProjectVersions(f.WorkingDir)
412		if projectVersion != "" && projectVersion != "dev" {
413			// Is the binary newer than the project?
414			projectNeedsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(projectVersion, f.Version)
415			if err == nil && projectNeedsUpgrade {
416				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
417					"\n💡 Project skills from DDx v%s (you have %s). Run 'ddx init --force' to update.\n",
418					strings.TrimPrefix(projectVersion, "v"),
419					f.Version)
420			}
421		}
422	
423		// Check plugin staleness (reuse ddx outdated logic)
424		f.displayPluginStalenessHints(cmd)
425	}
426	
427	// displayPluginStalenessHints reads the plugin version cache and shows hints for
428	// any installed plugins that have a newer version available.
429	func (f *CommandFactory) displayPluginStalenessHints(cmd *cobra.Command) {
430		state, err := registry.LoadState()
431		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
432			return
433		}
434	
435		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
436		if err := cache.Load(); err != nil {
437			return // no cache yet — check runs in background, hints appear next time
438		}
439	
440		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
441			latestVersion, ok := cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name]
442			if !ok {
443				continue
444			}
445			needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(entry.Version, latestVersion)
446			if err == nil && needsUpgrade {
447				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
448					"\n💡 %s %s installed, %s available. Run 'ddx install %s' to update.\n",
449					entry.Name, entry.Version, latestVersion, entry.Name)
450			}
451		}
452	}
453	
454	// refreshPluginVersionCache fetches the latest version for each installed plugin
455	// from GitHub and writes the result to the plugin cache. Called from the
456	// background update goroutine — failures are silent.
457	func (f *CommandFactory) refreshPluginVersionCache() {
458		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
459		_ = cache.Load() // ignore missing-file error
460	
461		if !cache.IsExpired() {
462			return
463		}
464	
465		state, err := registry.LoadState()
466		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
467			return
468		}
469	
470		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
471		updated := false
472		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
473			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
474			if err != nil {
475				continue
476			}
477			release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source)
478			if err != nil {
479				continue
480			}
481			latestVersion := strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
482			cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name] = latestVersion
483			updated = true
484		}
485	
486		if updated {
487			cache.Data.LastCheck = time.Now()
488			_ = cache.Save()
489		}
490	}

592	func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
593		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
594			return
595		}
596		if f.Commit == "" || f.Commit == "unknown" {
597			return
598		}
599	
600		repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
601		if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
602			return
603		}
604	
605		originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
606		if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
607			return
608		}
609	
610		buildSHA, err := gitResolveCommit(repoRoot, f.Commit)
611		if err != nil {
612			return
613		}
614		headSHA, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
615		if err != nil {
616			return
617		}
618	
619		if err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), repoRoot, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", buildSHA, headSHA).Run(); err != nil {
620			return
621		}
622	
623		aheadCount, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-list", "--count", buildSHA+".."+headSHA)
624		if err != nil || aheadCount == "0" {
625			return
626		}
627	
628		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
629			"WARNING: installed ddx is built from %s; source tree HEAD is %s (%s commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.\n",
630			buildSHA, headSHA, aheadCount)
631	}
632	
633	func gitCommandOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
634		out, err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).Output()
635		if err != nil {
636			return "", err
637		}
638		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
639	}
640	
641	func gitResolveCommit(dir, commit string) (string, error) {
642		return gitCommandOutput(dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", commit+"^{commit}")
643	}
644	
645	func isDDXOriginURL(url string) bool {
646		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(url), "documentdrivendx/ddx")
647	}
648	
649	// resolveAgentSession looks up a session by ID from the agent session log.
650	// Returns nil if the session is not found or cannot be read.
651	func (f *CommandFactory) resolveAgentSession(sessionID string) *agent.SessionEntry {
652		if sessionID == "" {
653			return nil
654		}
655	
656		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(f.WorkingDir)
657		if err != nil {
658			return nil
659		}
660	
661		sessionLogDir := ".ddx/agent-logs"
662		if cfg.Agent != nil && cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir != "" {
663			sessionLogDir = cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir
664		}
665	
666		logDir := agent.ResolveLogDir(f.WorkingDir, sessionLogDir)
667		idx, ok, err := agent.FindSessionIndex(logDir, sessionID)
668		if err != nil {
669			return nil
670		}
671		if ok {
672			entry := agent.SessionIndexEntryToLegacy(idx)
673			bodies := agent.LoadSessionBodies(f.WorkingDir, idx)
674			entry.Prompt = bodies.Prompt
675			entry.Response = bodies.Response
676			entry.Stderr = bodies.Stderr
677			return &entry
678		}
679		return nil
680	}

33	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentCommand() *cobra.Command {
34		cmd := &cobra.Command{
35			Use:   "agent",
36			Short: "Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging",
37			Long: `Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.
38	
39	Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
40	token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.
41	
42	The embedded DDx agent harness is named 'agent' and is always available without
43	installing external binaries. Use --harness agent or --profile cheap to route to it.
44	
45	Profile routing (--profile default|cheap|fast|smart) selects the best available harness
46	and model automatically. Workflow tools should prefer --profile over --harness to
47	stay decoupled from harness installation details.
48	
49	Examples:
50	  ddx agent run --profile default --prompt task.md
51	  ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt task.md
52	  ddx agent run --profile smart --prompt task.md
53	  ddx agent run --profile smart --model gpt-5.4   # explicit override; avoid by default
54	  ddx agent run --harness agent --prompt task.md

1486		spec, dispatch, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd, treatPassthroughAsOpaque)

1489		}
1490		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(spec.ProjectRoot, f.WorkingDir)
1491		spec.ProjectRoot = projectRoot
1492		if tryTargetBeadID != "" {
1493			// `ddx try <bead-id>` is a single-bead one-shot: target one bead and
1494			// exit after one attempt regardless of CLI flag state.
1495			spec.Mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
1496			spec.IdleInterval = executeloop.Duration{}
1497		}
1498	
1499		// Zero-config auto-route: when the operator passes no routing flags and
1500		// the project has no .ddx/config.yaml routing block, dispatch flows
1501		// through fizeau's provider resolution. Provider configuration and any
1502		// "no providers configured" error reporting is fizeau's concern; ddx
1503		// does not read fizeau's config files.
1504		// ADR-022: queue work always runs inline. The legacy --local flag is
1505		// a deprecated no-op and the server-submit path is removed from this CLI
1506		// surface — server-spawned workers exec `ddx work` directly.
1507	
1508		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1509		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1510		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1511		// any bead status changes hands.

1518		}

1520		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1521	
1522		// Structured progress sink for this loop run. Events emitted at
1523		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end land here so
1524		// log aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, `ddx server workers log`)
1525		// can parse the same JSONL envelope used by harness session logs.
1526		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"path/filepath"

8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
