12	)
13	
14	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted is an end-to-end integration test that
15	// drives runAgentExecuteBead via a cobra Command.Execute call and asserts that
16	// routing evidence (resolved_provider, resolved_model, route_reason) is persisted
17	// as a kind:routing bead event in the bead store.
18	//
19	// This test exercises the full pipeline:
20	//
21	//	CLI flags → ExecuteBeadOptions → AgentRunner.Run → appendBeadRoutingEvidence → bead store
22	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted(t *testing.T) {
23		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
24			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
25			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222", // agent produced a commit
26			mergeErr:    nil,
27		}
28		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
29			ExitCode:        0,
30			Harness:         "mock-harness",
31			Provider:        "test-provider",
32			Model:           "test-model-7b",
33			RouteReason:     "catalog-match",
34			ResolvedBaseURL: "http://test.example.com/v1",
35		}}
36		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
37	
38		// Drive the full command via cobra Command.Execute — not by calling internal
39		// functions directly. This exercises the CLI flag → ExecuteBeadOptions path.
40		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
41	
42		// Sanity-check: the command itself succeeded.
43		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
44		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
45		assert.Equal(t, "test-provider", res.Provider)
46		assert.Equal(t, "test-model-7b", res.Model)
47	
48		// Read routing events directly from the bead store that runAgentExecuteBead
49		// created (backed by f.WorkingDir/.ddx). This verifies end-to-end persistence,
50		// not just that appendBeadRoutingEvidence was called in isolation.
51		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, ".ddx"))
52		routingEvents, err := store.EventsByKind("my-bead", "routing")
53		require.NoError(t, err)
54		require.Len(t, routingEvents, 1, "expected exactly one kind:routing event")
55	
56		evt := routingEvents[0]
57		assert.Equal(t, "routing", evt.Kind)
58		assert.Equal(t, "ddx", evt.Actor)
59		assert.Equal(t, "ddx agent execute-bead", evt.Source)
60		assert.NotEmpty(t, evt.Summary)
61	
62		// Parse the JSON body and assert all three required routing fields.
63		var body struct {
64			ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
65			ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
66			RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
67			BaseURL          string `json:"base_url"`
68		}
69		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(evt.Body), &body),
70			"routing event body should be valid JSON: %s", evt.Body)
71		assert.Equal(t, "test-provider", body.ResolvedProvider)
72		assert.Equal(t, "test-model-7b", body.ResolvedModel)
73		assert.Equal(t, "catalog-match", body.RouteReason)
74		assert.Equal(t, "http://test.example.com/v1", body.BaseURL)
75	}
76	
77	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceProviderFallsBackToHarness verifies that when
78	// the runner returns an empty Provider, the routing event's resolved_provider
79	// falls back to the harness name (as implemented in appendBeadRoutingEvidence).
80	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceProviderFallsBackToHarness(t *testing.T) {
81		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
82			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
83			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
84		}
85		// Runner returns no Provider — harness should be used as resolved_provider.
86		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
87			ExitCode:    0,
88			Harness:     "fallback-harness",
89			Provider:    "", // empty — triggers harness fallback
90			Model:       "fallback-model",
91			RouteReason: "first-available",
92		}}
93		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
94	
95		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
96		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
97	
98		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, ".ddx"))
99		routingEvents, err := store.EventsByKind("my-bead", "routing")
100		require.NoError(t, err)
101		require.Len(t, routingEvents, 1)
102	
103		var body struct {
104			ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
105			ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
106			RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
107		}
108		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(routingEvents[0].Body), &body))
109		// Provider was empty, so resolved_provider falls back to harness.
110		assert.Equal(t, "fallback-harness", body.ResolvedProvider)
111		assert.Equal(t, "fallback-model", body.ResolvedModel)
112		assert.Equal(t, "first-available", body.RouteReason)
113	}
114	
115	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceNoEvidence verifies that routing evidence is
116	// persisted even when the agent exits without commits or rationale.
117	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceNoEvidence(t *testing.T) {
118		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
119			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
120			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // same rev — no commits made
121			wtDirty:     false,
122		}
123		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
124			ExitCode:    0,
125			Harness:     "mock-harness",
126			Provider:    "no-change-provider",
127			Model:       "no-change-model",
128			RouteReason: "direct-override",
129		}}
130		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
131	
132		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
133		require.Equal(t, "no-evidence", res.Outcome)
134	
135		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, ".ddx"))
136		routingEvents, err := store.EventsByKind("my-bead", "routing")
137		require.NoError(t, err)
138		require.Len(t, routingEvents, 1, "routing event should be persisted even on no-evidence outcome")
139	
140		var body struct {
141			ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
142			ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
143			RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
144		}
145		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(routingEvents[0].Body), &body))
146		assert.Equal(t, "no-change-provider", body.ResolvedProvider)
147		assert.Equal(t, "no-change-model", body.ResolvedModel)
148		assert.Equal(t, "direct-override", body.RouteReason)
149	}

942	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the thin worker: it creates an isolated worktree,
943	// constructs the agent prompt from bead context, runs the agent harness,
944	// synthesizes a commit if the agent left uncommitted changes, then cleans up
945	// the worktree and returns the result. It classifies outcomes as exactly one
946	// of:
947	//
948	//   - task_succeeded: agent exited 0 and produced one or more commits
949	//   - task_failed:    agent exited non-zero
950	//   - task_no_changes: agent exited 0, made no commits, and wrote a rationale
951	//   - task_no_evidence: agent exited 0 but made no commits and wrote no rationale
952	//
953	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, preserve-ref management, and orphan
954	// recovery are the parent's responsibility (see LandBeadResult, RecoverOrphans).
955	//
956	// Agent dispatch: production callers leave runtime.Service and runtime.AgentRunner
957	// nil. ExecuteBeadWithConfig constructs a fresh agentlib.FizeauService from
958	// projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir and dispatches via RunViaServiceWith.
959	// Tests may set runtime.AgentRunner to inject a fake that returns canned
960	// Result values; when set, it takes precedence over the service path.
961	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
962		if ctx == nil {
963			ctx = context.Background()
964		}
965		if runtime.WorkerID == "" {
966			runtime.WorkerID = os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID")
967		}
968	
969		resourceChecker := runtime.ResourceChecker
970		if resourceChecker == nil {
971			resourceChecker = NewExecutionResourceChecker(projectRoot, gitOps)
972		}
973		if _, err := resourceChecker.Check(ctx); err != nil {
974			var resourceErr *ResourceExhaustedError
975			if errors.As(err, &resourceErr) {
976				res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
977					BeadID:            beadID,
978					WorkerID:          runtime.WorkerID,
979					ExitCode:          1,
980					Error:             resourceErr.Error(),
981					Reason:            resourceErr.Error(),
982					Outcome:           ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
983					Status:            ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted,
984					ResourceExhausted: &resourceErr.Result,
985				}
986				res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
987				return res, nil
988			}
989			return nil, err
990		}
991	
992		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
993		wtPath := executeBeadWorktreePath(projectRoot, beadID, attemptID)
994	
995		// Serialize the parent-repo mutating pre-dispatch sequence (tracker
996		// commit, caller-dirt checkpoint, base resolution, worktree creation)
997		// under the tracker lock. Without this, concurrent workers race on the
998		// parent's HEAD ref between unlocked CommitTracker and SynthesizeCommit
999		// and surface as "cannot lock ref 'HEAD': is at X but expected Y" CAS
1000		// failures (regression: concurrent `ddx work` against the same project).
1001		// The lock is held only across the brief pre-dispatch sequence (~1-2s);
1002		// agent execution that follows is fully parallel.
1003		var baseRev string
1004		if err := withTrackerLock(projectRoot, func() error {
1005			if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(wtPath), 0o755); mkErr != nil {
1006				return fmt.Errorf("creating execute-bead worktree parent dir: %w", mkErr)
1007			}
1008			// Commit beads.jsonl before spawning worktree so the worktree snapshot
1009			// includes any bead metadata updates (e.g. spec-id).
1010			if err := commitTrackerLocked(projectRoot); err != nil {
1011				return err
1012			}
1013			// Checkpoint any remaining caller dirt as a real commit on the current
1014			// branch (FEAT-012 §22, US-126 AC#1). Use a temp-index commit-tree path
1015			// here so parent checkout hooks and runtime artifacts cannot fail the
1016			// dispatch before the isolated worker worktree exists.
1017			if _, err := checkpointPreDispatchDirt(projectRoot, attemptID); err != nil {
1018				return fmt.Errorf("pre-execute-bead checkpoint: %w", err)
1019			}
1020			// Resolve base revision after the tracker + checkpoint commits.
1021			rev, err := resolveBase(gitOps, projectRoot, runtime.FromRev)

23	// Exit codes for ddx try terminal outcomes. These are specific to ddx try
24	// and intentionally do not reuse the generic ExitCode* constants because
25	// the bead-execution outcome vocabulary is distinct from generic CLI errors.
26	const (

29		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success

32	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
33	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
34	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
35	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
36	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
37		cmd := &cobra.Command{
38			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
39			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
40			SilenceUsage: true,
41			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
42	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
43	
44	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
45	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
46	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
47	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
48	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
49	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
50	
51	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
52	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
53	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
54	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
55	
56	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
57	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
58	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
59	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
60	
61	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
62	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
63	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
64	
65	Exit codes:
66	  0  success or already_satisfied
67	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
68	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
69	`,

72	
73	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model

92		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")

104		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {

243				loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{

286				}
287				if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {

289					// the server-side evaluateGatesAndSubmit pattern. BuildLandingGateContext
290					// pins ResultRev to a transient ref and checks out an ephemeral
291					// worktree; failures are soft-logged and gate eval is skipped.
292					buildGateCtx := agent.BuildLandingGateContext
293					if f.tryBuildGateContextOverride != nil {

295					}
296					wt, ids, gateCleanup, ctxErr := buildGateCtx(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
297					if ctxErr != nil {

299					}

302					}

312								fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ddx: warning: preserving result ref %s failed: %v\n", preserveRef, upErr)
313							} else {
314								res.PreserveRef = preserveRef
315							}
316							res.Outcome = "preserved"
317							if ratchetFailed {
318								res.Reason = agent.RatchetPreserveReason
319							} else {
320								res.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
321							}
322							res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
323							res.Detail = agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
324							_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
325							return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
326						}

331						ctx, projectRoot, targetBead, res,
332						func(req agent.LandRequest) (*agent.LandResult, error) { return localCoord.Submit(req) },
333						bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
334						resolveClaimAssignee(), "ddx try",

336					)

340					} else {
341						agent.MarkResultLandError(projectRoot, res, landErr)
342						_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
343						return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
344					}
345				} else if res != nil && (res.Outcome == agent.ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed || res.ExitCode != 0) {
346					if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
347						res.Outcome = "preserved"
348					} else {
349						res.Outcome = "error"
350					}
351					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
352				} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
353					res.Outcome = "no-changes"
354					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
355				}
356				tierStr := ""
357				if inferredTier != "" {
358					tierStr = string(inferredTier)
359				}
360				return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, tierStr), nil
361			})
362		}
363	
364		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
365			Store:    forcedStore,
366			Executor: executor,
367			Reviewer: reviewer,
368		}
369	
370		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
371			Assignee: resolveClaimAssignee(),
372			Harness:  harness,
373			Model:    model,
374			Provider: provider,
375			ModelRef: modelRef,
376			Profile:  profile,
377			Effort:   effort,
378		}
379		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
380		if err != nil {
381			return fmt.Errorf("load resolved config: %w", err)
382		}
383		if _, err := resourceChecker.Check(cmd.Context()); err != nil {
384			if resErr, ok := err.(*agent.ResourceExhaustedError); ok && resErr != nil {
385				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), resErr.Error())
386			}
387			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: err.Error()}
388		}
389	
390		cliLandingOps := agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
391		var qualityRunner agent.AgentRunner
392		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
393			qualityRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
394		}
395		lintHook := agent.NewPreDispatchLintHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner)
396		triageHook := agent.NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner, nil)
397		result, runErr := worker.Run(cmd.Context(), rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
398			Mode:                  executeloop.ModeOnce,
399			Log:                   cmd.OutOrStdout(),
400			EventSink:             loopSink,
401			WorkerID:              resolveClaimAssignee(),
402			ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
403			ResourceChecker:       resourceChecker,
404			SessionID:             loopSessionID,
405			PreClaimHook:          buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps),
406			PreDispatchLintHook:   lintHook,
407			PostAttemptTriageHook: triageHook,
408			NoReview:              noReview,
409		})
410		if runErr != nil {
411			if (errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(runErr, context.DeadlineExceeded)) && result != nil && len(result.Results) > 0 {
412				writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.Results[0])
413			}
414			return runErr
415		}
416	
417		// Report outcome.
418		if result.NoReadyWork {
419			// Bead was not in the execution-ready queue even after pre-flight passed.
420			// This can happen if a heartbeat re-appeared just before claim, making it
421			// not stale-enough for reclaim. Surface a clear message.
422			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not execution-ready (it may be actively claimed by another worker): %s\n", beadID)
423			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
424		}
425	
426		if len(result.Results) == 0 {
427			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
428		}
429		report := result.Results[0]
430		writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), report)
431		if intent := escalation.ParseExecutionHint(target); intent.Source == escalation.ExecutionIntentSourceBeadHint && intent.RequestedTier != "" {
432			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "routing intent: tier=%s source=%s\n", intent.RequestedTier, intent.Source)
433		}
434	
435		return tryExitCodeForStatus(report.Status)
436	}
437	
438	func writeTryResult(w io.Writer, report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
439		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nbead: %s\nstatus: %s\n", report.BeadID, report.Status)
440		if report.Detail != "" {
441			fmt.Fprintf(w, "detail: %s\n", report.Detail)
442		}
443		if report.ResultRev != "" {
444			fmt.Fprintf(w, "result_rev: %s\n", report.ResultRev)
445		}
446		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
447			fmt.Fprintf(w, "preserve_ref: %s\n", report.PreserveRef)
448		}
449	}
450	
451	// tryExitCodeForStatus maps a bead execution status string to the ddx try exit code contract.
452	func tryExitCodeForStatus(status string) error {
453		switch status {
454		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
455			return nil // exit 0
456		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
457			agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced:
458			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitPreservedOrNone, Message: ""}
459		default:
460			// execution_failed and all other terminal non-success statuses
461			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
462		}
463	}
464	
465	// unmetDeps returns the IDs of deps that are not yet closed, in the order
466	// they appear in the bead's dependency list.
467	func unmetDeps(store *bead.Store, b *bead.Bead) []string {
468		depIDs := b.DepIDs()
469		if len(depIDs) == 0 {
470			return nil
471		}
472		var unmet []string
473		for _, depID := range depIDs {
474			dep, err := store.Get(depID)
475			if err != nil || dep.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
476				unmet = append(unmet, depID)
477			}
478		}
479		return unmet
480	}
481	
482	// singleBeadStore wraps a *bead.Store and overrides ReadyExecution to return
483	// only a single target bead. All other operations are delegated unchanged.
484	// This allows the execute-bead worker to treat the forced bead as if it were
485	// the only entry in the execution-ready queue.
486	type singleBeadStore struct {
487		*bead.Store
488		target bead.Bead
489	}
490	
491	func (s *singleBeadStore) ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error) {
492		return []bead.Bead{s.target}, nil
493	}

29	func (e *blockingTryExecutor) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
30		e.once.Do(func() { close(e.started) })
31		<-ctx.Done()
32		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
33			BeadID:    beadID,
34			Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
35			Detail:    ctx.Err().Error(),
36			BaseRev:   "feedface",
37			ResultRev: "feedface",
38		}, ctx.Err()
39	}

41	func runInterruptedTryCommand(t *testing.T) (*bead.Store, string, error) {
42		t.Helper()
43	
44		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
45		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
46		env.CreateDefaultConfig()
47		store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
48		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
49		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
50			ID:    "try-interrupt-001",
51			Title: "Interrupted try bead",
52		}))
53	
54		factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
55		runner := &blockingTryExecutor{started: make(chan struct{})}
56		factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
57		factory.tryExecutorOverride = runner
58		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
59	
60		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
61		root.SetContext(ctx)
62	
63		var buf bytes.Buffer
64		root.SetOut(&buf)
65		root.SetErr(&buf)
66		root.SetArgs([]string{"try", "try-interrupt-001", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing"})
67	
68		done := make(chan error, 1)
69		go func() {
70			done <- root.Execute()
71		}()
72	
73		select {
74		case <-runner.started:
75		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
76			t.Fatal("try executor did not start")
77		}
78		cancel()
79	
80		var err error
81		select {
82		case err = <-done:
83		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
84			t.Fatal("try command did not return after cancel")
85		}
86	
87		return store, buf.String(), err
88	}

329	// reach the executor. We capture the resolved config in the stub executor.
330	func TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough(t *testing.T) {
331		// Isolate from ~/.config/fizeau/config.yaml so the pre-dispatch lint hook
332		// does not dispatch to real providers (HTTP or exec-based) during the test.
333		t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())
334		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
335		store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
336		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
337		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
338			ID:    "flag-bead-001",
339			Title: "Flag plumbing bead",
340		}))
341	
342		var capturedBeadID string
343		stubExecutor := agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
344			capturedBeadID = beadID
345			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
346				BeadID: beadID,
347				Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
348			}, nil
349		})
350	
351		factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
352		factory.tryExecutorOverride = stubExecutor
353		factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
354		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
355	
356		// The --harness and --model flags are passthrough — they reach the runtime
357		// config (CLIOverrides). Since the stub executor bypasses the real agent,
358		// we assert the flags are accepted without error (no flag-parsing failure)
359		// and the executor is invoked with the correct bead ID.
360		out, err := executeCommand(root, "try", "flag-bead-001",
361			"--harness=codex", "--model=gpt-5.4-mini", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
362		require.NoError(t, err, "ddx try with routing flags must not fail on flag parsing: %s", out)
363		assert.Equal(t, "flag-bead-001", capturedBeadID,
364			"executor must receive the correct bead ID regardless of routing flags")
365		// Verify the flags appear in help (AC #7 surface check).
366		tryCmd, _, findErr := root.Find([]string{"try"})
367		require.NoError(t, findErr)
368		assert.NotNil(t, tryCmd.Flags().Lookup("harness"), "try must expose --harness flag")
369		assert.NotNil(t, tryCmd.Flags().Lookup("model"), "try must expose --model flag")
370		assert.NotNil(t, tryCmd.Flags().Lookup("profile"), "try must expose --profile flag")
371		assert.NotNil(t, tryCmd.Flags().Lookup("provider"), "try must expose --provider flag")
372		assert.NotNil(t, tryCmd.Flags().Lookup("no-review"), "try must expose --no-review flag")
373		assert.NotNil(t, tryCmd.Flags().Lookup("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing"), "try must expose the break-glass acknowledgement flag")
374		assert.NotNil(t, tryCmd.Flags().Lookup("review-harness"), "try must expose --review-harness flag")
375		assert.NotNil(t, tryCmd.Flags().Lookup("review-model"), "try must expose --review-model flag")
376	}

378	// TestTry_HooksWired verifies that ddx try wires the pre-dispatch lint and
379	// post-attempt triage hooks into ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, and that both hooks
380	// route through the shared runner override seam.
381	func TestTry_HooksWired(t *testing.T) {
382		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
383		skillPath := env.Dir + "/.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle"
384		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillPath, 0o755))
385		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(skillPath+"/SKILL.md", []byte("lint + triage"), 0o644))
386		store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
387		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
388		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
389			ID:    "hook-bead-001",
390			Title: "Hook wiring bead",
391		}))
392	
393		runner := &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
394		factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
395		factory.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
396		factory.tryExecutorOverride = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
397			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
398				BeadID:    beadID,
399				Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
400				Detail:    "nothing changed",
401				BaseRev:   "feedface",
402				ResultRev: "feedface",
403				SessionID: "sess-hook",
404			}, nil
405		})
406	
407		out, err := executeCommand(
408			factory.NewRootCommand(),
409			"try",
410			"hook-bead-001",
411		)
412		require.Error(t, err)
413		assert.Contains(t, out, "bead:", "try command should still render the terminal report")
414		assert.Equal(t, []string{"bead-lifecycle-lint", "bead-lifecycle-triage"}, runner.promptSource)
415	
416		events, err := store.Events("hook-bead-001")
417		require.NoError(t, err)
418		var lintSeen, triageSeen bool
419		for _, ev := range events {
420			switch ev.Kind {
421			case "bead-quality.lint":
422				lintSeen = true
423			case "bead-quality.triage":
424				triageSeen = true
425			}
426		}
427		assert.True(t, lintSeen, "try must emit the lint event when the hook is wired")
428		assert.True(t, triageSeen, "try must emit the triage event when the hook is wired")
429	}

94	func TestParseExecuteLoopSpec_RateLimitMaxWait(t *testing.T) {
95		tests := []struct {
96			name string
97			set  string
98			want time.Duration
99		}{
100			{name: "default", want: agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget},
101			{name: "explicit positive", set: "90s", want: 90 * time.Second},
102			{name: "zero uses runtime default", set: "0", want: 0},
103			{name: "negative disables retry", set: "-1s", want: -1 * time.Second},
104		}
105	
106		for _, tt := range tests {
107			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
108				dir := t.TempDir()
109				root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
110				workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
111				require.NoError(t, err)
112				if tt.set != "" {
113					require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("rate-limit-max-wait", tt.set))
114				}
115	
116				spec, _, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(workCmd, true)
117				require.NoError(t, err)
118				assert.Equal(t, tt.want, spec.RateLimitMaxWait.Duration)
119			})
120		}
121	}

212	// TestWorkPassthroughNotValidated is the primary AC test: ddx work must NOT
213	// call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService. When an unknown harness is provided
214	// and no ready beads exist, work must succeed with no_ready_work rather than
215	// failing on harness validation. This proves harness/provider/model are opaque
216	// passthrough — ddx work does not validate or branch on their string values.
217	func TestWorkPassthroughNotValidated(t *testing.T) {
218		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
219		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
220	
221		// "bogus_harness_xyz_notreal" does not exist in any harness registry.
222		// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService would fail with "unknown harness: ..."
223		// if called. ddx work must skip that call and proceed to the queue scan,
224		// finding no ready beads and returning successfully.
225		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work",
226			"--json", "--once",
227			"--harness=bogus_harness_xyz_notreal",
228		)
229		require.NoError(t, err,
230			"ddx work must not fail on unknown harness when there are no ready beads; "+
231				"harness is opaque passthrough, not validated by ddx work")
232	
233		var res struct {
234			NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
235		}
236		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
237		assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork,
238			"ddx work with no ready beads must report no_ready_work=true")
239	}

19	// NowFunc allows tests to override time.Now for deterministic PreserveRef output.
20	var NowFunc = time.Now
21	
22	// PreserveRef builds the documented hidden ref for a preserved iteration.
23	func PreserveRef(beadID, baseRev string) string {
24		shortSHA := baseRev
25		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
26			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
27		}
28		timestamp := NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
29		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, timestamp, shortSHA)
30	}
31	
32	// GateCheckResult records the outcome of one required execution gate.
33	type GateCheckResult struct {
34		DefinitionID string `json:"definition_id"`
35		Required     bool   `json:"required"`
36		ExitCode     int    `json:"exit_code"`
37		// Status is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
38		Status string `json:"status"`
39		Stdout string `json:"stdout,omitempty"`
40		Stderr string `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
41		// Ratchet captures the ratchet evaluation when the gate declared
42		// thresholds. Populated for both pass and fail decisions so HELIX can
43		// distinguish ratchet outcomes from generic command failures.
44		Ratchet *RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// RatchetEvidence is the machine-readable record of one ratchet evaluation
48	// performed before landing. Threshold/Observed carry the authored ratchet
49	// and the observed value; Decision is "pass" or "fail"; Reason provides a
50	// short explanation of what was compared ("observed 310 ms > ratchet 250 ms").
51	type RatchetEvidence struct {
52		DefinitionID string  `json:"definition_id"`
53		MetricID     string  `json:"metric_id,omitempty"`
54		Comparison   string  `json:"comparison,omitempty"`
55		Threshold    float64 `json:"threshold"`
56		Observed     float64 `json:"observed"`
57		Unit         string  `json:"unit,omitempty"`
58		Decision     string  `json:"decision"`
59		Reason       string  `json:"reason,omitempty"`
60	}
61	
62	// RatchetPreserveReason marks a landing that was preserved because a declared
63	// ratchet was not met. Callers match on this exact string (via the landing
64	// result's Reason field) to bucket ratchet-preserved attempts apart from
65	// generic gate failures.
66	const RatchetPreserveReason = "ratchet miss"
67	
68	// executeBeadChecks is the machine-readable schema for checks.json.
69	// Written by the orchestrator when gate evaluation runs.
70	type executeBeadChecks struct {
71		AttemptID       string            `json:"attempt_id"`
72		EvaluatedAt     time.Time         `json:"evaluated_at"`
73		Summary         string            `json:"summary"`
74		Results         []GateCheckResult `json:"results"`
75		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
76		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
77	}
78	
79	// OrchestratorGitOps abstracts the git operations needed by the parent-side
80	// orchestrator for preserving worker results under iteration refs.
81	//
82	// NOTE: The Merge(dir, rev) method that existed here before the land
83	// coordinator redesign has been DELETED. All target-branch writes now flow
84	// through Land() in execute_bead_land.go and its per-project serialized
85	// coordinator. See ddx-8746d8a6 / ddx-e14efc58 / ddx-6aa50e57 for the
86	// rationale: the old path produced "chore: checkpoint before merge" noise
87	// and workers racing on the same projectRoot could corrupt each other's
88	// intermediate state. Land() serializes through a single goroutine and
89	// uses `git merge --no-ff` when the target has advanced, so the worker's
90	// commit is never rewritten and replay sees the same inputs it originally saw.
91	//
92	// The LandingAdvancer field on BeadLandingOptions is the coordinator
93	// injection point for LandBeadResult callers that need to ff the target
94	// branch. When LandingAdvancer is nil (the interactive single-bead CLI),
95	// LandBeadResult falls back to preserving the result under
96	// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... rather than modifying the target branch.
97	type OrchestratorGitOps interface {
98		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
99	}
100	
101	// RealOrchestratorGitOps implements OrchestratorGitOps via os/exec git commands.
102	type RealOrchestratorGitOps struct{}
103	
104	// UpdateRef updates a git ref to point at sha.
105	func (r *RealOrchestratorGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
106		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
107		if err != nil {
108			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
109		}
110		return nil
111	}
112	
113	// BeadLandingOptions controls how the orchestrator lands a completed worker result.
114	type BeadLandingOptions struct {
115		// NoMerge skips the land step and preserves the result under
116		// refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... instead.
117		NoMerge bool
118	
119		// WtPath is the path to a worktree checked out at ResultRev, used for gate
120		// evaluation. When empty, gate evaluation is skipped (the worktree has
121		// typically been cleaned up by the time the orchestrator runs).
122		WtPath string
123	
124		// GovernIDs are the governing artifact IDs to use for gate evaluation.
125		// Only used when WtPath is non-empty. Typically extracted from the worker's
126		// manifest artifact via ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest.
127		GovernIDs []string
128	
129		// ChecksArtifactPath is the absolute path to write checks.json. Optional.
130		ChecksArtifactPath string
131		// ChecksArtifactRel is the relative path stored in the result for checks.json.
132		ChecksArtifactRel string
133	
134		// LandingAdvancer, when non-nil, replaces the old in-process Merge step
135		// with the coordinator-pattern Land() call. The callback is expected to
136		// run fetch → (ff or merge) → push serialized against other submissions
137		// for the same projectRoot. When nil, LandBeadResult falls back to
138		// preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/...
139		// rather than touching the target branch.
140		LandingAdvancer func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error)
141	}
142	
143	// BeadLandingResult records the outcome of the orchestrator's landing step.
144	type BeadLandingResult struct {
145		// Outcome is one of: "merged", "preserved", "no-changes".
146		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
147		// Reason is a human-readable explanation of the outcome.
148		Reason string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
149		// PreserveRef is set when the result was preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/...
150		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
151		// GateResults holds the outcome of each required execution gate.
152		GateResults []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
153		// RequiredExecSummary is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
154		RequiredExecSummary string `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
155		// ChecksFile is the relative path to checks.json when gate results were written.
156		ChecksFile string `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
157		// RatchetEvidence aggregates every ratchet-evaluated gate's evidence for
158		// the attempt. Surfaced separately from GateResults so HELIX and other
159		// consumers can tell apart ratchet outcomes from generic gate failures.
160		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
161		// RatchetSummary is "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
162		RatchetSummary string `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
163	}
164	
165	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
166	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
167	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
168	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
169	//
170	// Outcome rules:
171	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
172	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
173	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
174	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
175	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
176	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
177		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
178	
179		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
180		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
181		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
182		agentFailed := res.ExitCode != 0 || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
183		if agentFailed && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
184			landing.Outcome = "error"
185			switch {
186			case res.Reason != "":
187				landing.Reason = res.Reason
188			case res.Error != "":
189				landing.Reason = res.Error
190			default:
191				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
192			}
193			return landing, nil
194		}
195	
196		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
197		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
198			if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence || res.FailureMode == FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced {
199				landing.Outcome = "no-evidence"
200				if res.Reason != "" {
201					landing.Reason = res.Reason
202				} else if res.Error != "" {
203					landing.Reason = res.Error
204				} else {
205					landing.Reason = "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt"
206				}
207				return landing, nil
208			}
209			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
210			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
211				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
212			} else {
213				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
214			}
215			return landing, nil
216		}
217	
218		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
219		if agentFailed {
220			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
221			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
222				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
223			}
224			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
225			landing.PreserveRef = ref
226			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
227			return landing, nil
228		}
229	
230		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
231		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
232		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting
233		// through the coordinator. It mutates res.GateResults / RequiredExecSummary
234		// / RatchetEvidence / RatchetSummary / ChecksFile in place; we mirror the
235		// outputs onto landing so ApplyLandingToResult can copy them back later
236		// without losing the existing landing.* contract.
237		anyGateFailed, anyRatchetFailed, gateErr := EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(
238			opts.WtPath, opts.GovernIDs, res, projectRoot,
239			opts.ChecksArtifactPath, opts.ChecksArtifactRel,
240		)
241		if gateErr != nil {
242			return nil, gateErr
243		}
244		gateResults := res.GateResults
245		landing.GateResults = gateResults
246		landing.RequiredExecSummary = res.RequiredExecSummary
247		landing.RatchetEvidence = res.RatchetEvidence
248		landing.RatchetSummary = res.RatchetSummary
249		landing.ChecksFile = res.ChecksFile
250	
251		// Gate failed: preserve instead of merging. Ratchet misses get a
252		// dedicated reason so status/failure_mode classifiers can distinguish
253		// them from generic command failures.
254		if anyGateFailed {
255			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
256			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
257				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
258			}
259			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
260			landing.PreserveRef = ref
261			if anyRatchetFailed {
262				landing.Reason = RatchetPreserveReason
263			} else {
264				landing.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
265			}
266			return landing, nil
267		}
268	
269		// --no-merge: preserve unconditionally.
270		if opts.NoMerge {
271			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
272			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
273				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
274			}
275			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
276			landing.PreserveRef = ref
277			landing.Reason = "--no-merge specified"
278			return landing, nil
279		}
280	
281		// Default: land the worker's commits on the target branch. When a
282		// LandingAdvancer is provided (server coordinator / --local coordinator)
283		// it runs the fetch → (ff or merge) → push sequence serialized per
284		// projectRoot. When no advancer is provided, LandBeadResult falls back
285		// to preserving the result under refs/ddx/iterations/ — the interactive
286		// single-bead CLI path, which intentionally does NOT auto-advance the
287		// target branch (the operator moves the ref themselves).
288		if opts.LandingAdvancer != nil {
289			land, landErr := opts.LandingAdvancer(res)
290			if landErr != nil {
291				return nil, fmt.Errorf("land advancer: %w", landErr)
292			}
293			switch land.Status {
294			case "landed":
295				landing.Outcome = "merged"
296				if land.Merged {
297					landing.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
298				}
299				if land.NewTip != "" {
300					res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
301				}
302				if land.PushFailed {
303					landing.Reason = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
304				}
305			case "preserved":
306				landing.Outcome = "preserved"
307				landing.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
308				landing.Reason = land.Reason
309			case "no-changes":
310				landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
311				landing.Reason = land.Reason
312			default:
313				landing.Outcome = "preserved"
314				landing.Reason = "unknown land status: " + land.Status
315			}
316			return landing, nil
317		}
318	
319		// No advancer: preserve under refs/ddx/iterations/ as a safe fallback.
320		ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
321		if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
322			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref (no land advancer): %w", err)
323		}
324		landing.Outcome = "preserved"
325		landing.PreserveRef = ref
326		landing.Reason = "no land advancer configured"
327		return landing, nil
328	}
329	
330	// ApplyLandingToResult merges a BeadLandingResult's fields into an
331	// ExecuteBeadResult so callers can output a single unified record. It
332	// overwrites Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults,
333	// RequiredExecSummary, and ChecksFile based on the landing decision.
334	func ApplyLandingToResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, landing *BeadLandingResult) {
335		res.Outcome = landing.Outcome
336		res.Reason = landing.Reason
337		res.PreserveRef = landing.PreserveRef
338		res.GateResults = landing.GateResults
339		res.RequiredExecSummary = landing.RequiredExecSummary
340		res.ChecksFile = landing.ChecksFile
341		res.RatchetEvidence = landing.RatchetEvidence
342		res.RatchetSummary = landing.RatchetSummary
343		// Re-classify status based on landing outcome and reason.
344		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(landing.Outcome, res.ExitCode, landing.Reason)
345		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, landing.Reason, res.Error)
346		// Refine failure_mode with landing-level signals. A clean merge clears
347		// the field; merge conflict or gate failure overrides the worker-level
348		// classification; other preserved outcomes keep the worker's mode.
349		res.FailureMode = classifyLandingFailureMode(landing.Outcome, landing.Reason, landing.GateResults, res.FailureMode)
350		res.OrchestratorStatus = res.Status
351	}
352	
353	// ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest reads governing artifact IDs from a manifest
354	// JSON file at manifestAbs (absolute path). Returns nil when the file cannot
355	// be read. Callers use this to populate BeadLandingOptions.GovernIDs.
356	func ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest(manifestAbs string) []string {
357		if manifestAbs == "" {
358			return nil
359		}
360		type manifestShape struct {
361			Governing []struct {
362				ID string `json:"id"`
363			} `json:"governing"`
364		}
365		raw, err := os.ReadFile(manifestAbs)
366		if err != nil {
367			return nil
368		}
369		var m manifestShape
370		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &m); err != nil {
371			return nil
372		}
373		ids := make([]string, 0, len(m.Governing))
374		for _, g := range m.Governing {
375			if g.ID != "" {
376				ids = append(ids, g.ID)
377			}
378		}
379		return ids
380	}
381	
382	// RecoverOrphans removes orphaned execute-bead worktrees for a given bead ID.
383	// This is the parent's responsibility — call it before spawning new workers
384	// so stale worktrees from crashed previous attempts do not accumulate.
385	func RecoverOrphans(gitOps GitOps, workDir, beadID string) {
386		paths, err := gitOps.WorktreeList(workDir)
387		if err != nil {
388			return
389		}
390		// Match by basename prefix so orphans are found regardless of their parent
391		// directory (legacy .ddx/ vs new $TMPDIR/ddx-exec-wt/).
392		basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"
393		orphanRemoved := false
394		for _, p := range paths {
395			if strings.HasPrefix(filepath.Base(p), basenamePrefix) {
396				_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, p)
397				orphanRemoved = true
398			}
399		}
400		_ = gitOps.WorktreePrune(workDir)
401	
402		// Sweep stale run-state left by crashed workers. Per-attempt records are
403		// cleared individually so concurrent attempts for other beads remain visible.
404		if states, _ := ReadRunStates(workDir); len(states) > 0 {
405			for _, state := range states {
406				remove := orphanRemoved && state.BeadID == beadID
407				if !remove && state.BeadID == beadID {
408					remove = true
409				}
410				if !remove && state.WorktreePath != "" {
411					if _, err := os.Stat(state.WorktreePath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
412						remove = true
413					}
414				}
415				if remove {
416					_ = ClearRunStateAttempt(workDir, state.AttemptID)
417				}
418			}
419		}
420	}
421	
422	// evaluateRequiredGates resolves graph-authored execution documents that are
423	// required and linked to any of the governing artifact IDs, then runs each
424	// one. When a gate declares a ratchet threshold, the command's numeric output
425	// is compared against the threshold after a successful exit; a ratchet miss
426	// demotes the gate status to "fail" and records the evidence the orchestrator
427	// surfaces as part of its landing decision.
428	//
429	// Returns: gate results (one per candidate), anyFailed (true if any gate's
430	// final status is "fail"), anyRatchetFailed (true if any failure was caused
431	// by a ratchet miss rather than a non-zero exit), and any fatal error from
432	// walking the graph (soft errors are swallowed).
433	func evaluateRequiredGates(wtPath string, governingIDs []string) ([]GateCheckResult, bool, bool, error) {
434		if len(governingIDs) == 0 {
435			return nil, false, false, nil
436		}
437	
438		graph, err := docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig(wtPath)
439		if err != nil {
440			// Soft error: skip gate evaluation rather than blocking all landings.
441			return nil, false, false, nil
442		}
443	
444		governingSet := make(map[string]bool, len(governingIDs))
445		for _, id := range governingIDs {
446			governingSet[id] = true
447		}
448	
449		type execCandidate struct {
450			id         string
451			command    []string
452			cwd        string
453			comparison string
454			thresholds *docgraph.DocThresholds
455			metric     *docgraph.DocMetricSpec
456		}
457		var candidates []execCandidate
458		for _, doc := range graph.Documents {
459			if doc.ExecDef == nil || !doc.ExecDef.Required {
460				continue
461			}
462			ed := doc.ExecDef
463			if ed.Kind != "command" {
464				continue
465			}
466			if len(ed.Command) == 0 {
467				continue
468			}
469			linked := false
470			for _, dep := range doc.DependsOn {
471				if governingSet[dep] {
472					linked = true
473					break
474				}
475			}
476			if !linked {
477				for _, artID := range ed.ArtifactIDs {
478					if governingSet[artID] {
479						linked = true
480						break
481					}
482				}
483			}
484			if !linked {
485				continue
486			}
487			candidates = append(candidates, execCandidate{
488				id:         doc.ID,
489				command:    ed.Command,
490				cwd:        ed.Cwd,
491				comparison: ed.Comparison,
492				thresholds: ed.Thresholds,
493				metric:     ed.Metric,
494			})
495		}
496	
497		if len(candidates) == 0 {
498			return nil, false, false, nil
499		}
500	
501		anyFailed := false
502		anyRatchetFailed := false
503		results := make([]GateCheckResult, 0, len(candidates))
504		for _, c := range candidates {
505			cwd := wtPath
506			if c.cwd != "" {
507				if filepath.IsAbs(c.cwd) {
508					cwd = c.cwd
509				} else {
510					cwd = filepath.Join(wtPath, c.cwd)
511				}
512			}
513	
514			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Minute)
515			cmd := osexec.CommandContext(ctx, c.command[0], c.command[1:]...)
516			cmd.Dir = cwd
517			var stdoutBuf, stderrBuf strings.Builder
518			cmd.Stdout = &stdoutBuf
519			cmd.Stderr = &stderrBuf
520			runErr := cmd.Run()
521			cancel()
522	
523			stdoutStr := strings.TrimSpace(stdoutBuf.String())
524			stderrStr := strings.TrimSpace(stderrBuf.String())
525			gr := GateCheckResult{
526				DefinitionID: c.id,
527				Required:     true,
528				Stdout:       stdoutStr,
529				Stderr:       stderrStr,
530			}
531			if runErr != nil {
532				gr.ExitCode = 1
533				if exitErr, ok := runErr.(*osexec.ExitError); ok {
534					gr.ExitCode = exitErr.ExitCode()
535				}
536				gr.Status = "fail"
537				anyFailed = true
538			} else {
539				gr.Status = "pass"
540			}
541	
542			// Apply ratchet evaluation when thresholds are declared. Only runs
543			// when the command exited cleanly — a non-zero exit already failed
544			// the gate and there's no point parsing unreliable output.
545			if c.thresholds != nil && gr.Status == "pass" {
546				evidence := evaluateRatchet(c.id, c.comparison, c.thresholds, c.metric, stdoutStr, stderrStr)
547				gr.Ratchet = evidence
548				if evidence.Decision == "fail" {
549					gr.Status = "fail"
550					anyFailed = true
551					anyRatchetFailed = true
552				}
553			}
554			results = append(results, gr)
555		}
556	
557		return results, anyFailed, anyRatchetFailed, nil
558	}
559	
560	// evaluateRatchet parses the gate's observed value from stdout and compares it
561	// against the declared ratchet threshold. Always returns a non-nil evidence
562	// record so pass/fail results both carry machine-readable provenance.
563	func evaluateRatchet(definitionID, comparison string, thresholds *docgraph.DocThresholds, metric *docgraph.DocMetricSpec, stdout, stderr string) *RatchetEvidence {
564		if comparison == "" {
565			comparison = "lower-is-better"
566		}
567		unit := ""
568		metricID := ""
569		if metric != nil {
570			unit = metric.Unit
571			metricID = metric.MetricID
572		}
573		if unit == "" {
574			unit = thresholds.Unit
575		}
576		observed, parsedUnit, parsed := parseGateValue(stdout)
577		if !parsed {
578			// Fall back to stderr for tools that report numbers there.
579			observed, parsedUnit, parsed = parseGateValue(stderr)
580		}
581		if unit == "" {
582			unit = parsedUnit
583		}
584		evidence := &RatchetEvidence{
585			DefinitionID: definitionID,
586			MetricID:     metricID,
587			Comparison:   comparison,
588			Threshold:    thresholds.Ratchet,
589			Observed:     observed,
590			Unit:         unit,
591		}
592		if !parsed {
593			evidence.Decision = "fail"
594			evidence.Reason = "gate did not emit a parseable numeric value"
595			return evidence
596		}
597		passed := ratchetPasses(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed)
598		if passed {
599			evidence.Decision = "pass"
600			evidence.Reason = formatRatchetReason(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed, unit, true)
601		} else {
602			evidence.Decision = "fail"
603			evidence.Reason = formatRatchetReason(comparison, thresholds.Ratchet, observed, unit, false)
604		}
605		return evidence
606	}
607	
608	// ratchetPasses returns true when observed satisfies the ratchet constraint.
609	// lower-is-better: observed must be <= threshold. higher-is-better: observed
610	// must be >= threshold. Any other comparison string defaults to lower-is-better.
611	func ratchetPasses(comparison string, threshold, observed float64) bool {
612		if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
613			return observed >= threshold
614		}
615		return observed <= threshold
616	}
617	
618	func formatRatchetReason(comparison string, threshold, observed float64, unit string, passed bool) string {
619		cmpOp := "<="
620		if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
621			cmpOp = ">="
622		}
623		state := "pass"
624		if !passed {
625			state = "fail"
626			// flip operator for failures so the message reads like "observed 310 > ratchet 250"
627			if comparison == "higher-is-better" {
628				cmpOp = "<"
629			} else {
630				cmpOp = ">"
631			}
632		}
633		if unit != "" {
634			return fmt.Sprintf("%s: observed %g %s %s ratchet %g %s", state, observed, unit, cmpOp, threshold, unit)
635		}
636		return fmt.Sprintf("%s: observed %g %s ratchet %g", state, observed, cmpOp, threshold)
637	}
638	
639	// parseGateValue extracts a numeric observation from gate output. Accepts
640	// either a JSON object with a "value" (and optional "unit") field, or a
641	// trailing number with an optional unit suffix. Returns the value, the parsed
642	// unit (may be ""), and whether parsing succeeded.
643	func parseGateValue(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
644		text = strings.TrimSpace(text)
645		if text == "" {
646			return 0, "", false
647		}
648		if v, u, ok := parseGateJSON(text); ok {
649			return v, u, true
650		}
651		return parseGateText(text)
652	}
653	
654	var gateMeasurementPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(?:\s*)([a-zA-Z%/]+)?`)
655	
656	func parseGateJSON(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
657		var obj map[string]any
658		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(text), &obj); err != nil {
659			return 0, "", false
660		}
661		raw, ok := obj["value"]
662		if !ok {
663			return 0, "", false
664		}
665		unit, _ := obj["unit"].(string)
666		switch v := raw.(type) {
667		case float64:
668			return v, unit, true
669		case string:
670			if parsed, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64); err == nil {
671				return parsed, unit, true
672			}
673		}
674		return 0, "", false
675	}
676	
677	func parseGateText(text string) (float64, string, bool) {
678		// Take the last matching measurement so "result: 310 ms" still parses.
679		matches := gateMeasurementPattern.FindAllStringSubmatch(text, -1)
680		if len(matches) == 0 {
681			return 0, "", false
682		}
683		m := matches[len(matches)-1]
684		v, err := strconv.ParseFloat(m[1], 64)
685		if err != nil {
686			return 0, "", false
687		}
688		unit := ""
689		if len(m) >= 3 {
690			unit = strings.TrimSpace(m[2])
691		}
692		return v, unit, true
693	}
694	
695	// collectRatchetEvidence flattens the ratchet evidence from each gate result
696	// into a single slice the orchestrator stores on the landing record.
697	func collectRatchetEvidence(gateResults []GateCheckResult) []RatchetEvidence {
698		var out []RatchetEvidence
699		for _, gr := range gateResults {
700			if gr.Ratchet != nil {
701				out = append(out, *gr.Ratchet)
702			}
703		}
704		return out
705	}
706	
707	// summarizeRatchets returns "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
708	func summarizeRatchets(evidence []RatchetEvidence) string {
709		if len(evidence) == 0 {
710			return ""
711		}
712		for _, e := range evidence {
713			if e.Decision == "fail" {
714				return "fail"
715			}
716		}
717		return "pass"
718	}
719	
720	// summarizeGates returns the RequiredExecSummary string for the landing result.
721	func summarizeGates(results []GateCheckResult, anyFailed bool) string {
722		if len(results) == 0 {
723			return "skipped"
724		}
725		if anyFailed {
726			return "fail"
727		}
728		return "pass"
729	}

27-// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
28-// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
29-// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30-// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31-// returned to a caller.

39-	// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by

```go
func RecoverOrphans(gitOps GitOps, workDir, beadID string) {
```

**Parameters:**
- `gitOps GitOps` - abstracts git operations (WorktreeList, WorktreeRemove, WorktreePrune)
- `workDir string` - project work directory
- `beadID string` - bead identifier to recover orphans for

**What it does:** Removes orphaned execute-bead worktrees and stale run-state left by crashed workers. Matches worktree basenames by prefix (`ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"`), removes matched worktrees, prunes references, and sweeps per-attempt RunState records for the bead.

22	func (c *workResourceExhaustedChecker) Check(ctx context.Context) (agent.ExecutionResourceCheckResult, error) {
23		_ = ctx
24		n := atomic.AddInt32(&c.calls, 1)
25		failOn := c.failOn
26		if failOn == 0 {
27			failOn = 2
28		}
29		result := agent.ExecutionResourceCheckResult{
30			ProjectRoot: c.projectRoot,
31			TempRoot:    filepath.Join(c.projectRoot, ".ddx", "tmp"),
32			EvidenceRoots: []string{
33				filepath.Join(c.projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions"),
34			},
35		}
36		if n == failOn {
37			result.CleanupSummary = agent.ExecutionCleanupSummary{
38				ProjectRoot:                 c.projectRoot,
39				TempRoot:                    result.TempRoot,
40				ScannedTempDirs:             1,
41				RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs: 1,

43			}
44			return result, &agent.ResourceExhaustedError{
45				Detail: "temp root is full",

14	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted is an end-to-end integration test that
15	// drives runAgentExecuteBead via a cobra Command.Execute call and asserts that
16	// routing evidence (resolved_provider, resolved_model, route_reason) is persisted
17	// as a kind:routing bead event in the bead store.
18	//
19	// This test exercises the full pipeline:
20	//
21	//	CLI flags → ExecuteBeadOptions → AgentRunner.Run → appendBeadRoutingEvidence → bead store
22	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted(t *testing.T) {
23		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
24			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
25			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222", // agent produced a commit
26			mergeErr:    nil,
27		}
28		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
29			ExitCode:        0,
30			Harness:         "mock-harness",
31			Provider:        "test-provider",
32			Model:           "test-model-7b",
33			RouteReason:     "catalog-match",
34			ResolvedBaseURL: "http://test.example.com/v1",
35		}}
36		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
37	
38		// Drive the full command via cobra Command.Execute — not by calling internal
39		// functions directly. This exercises the CLI flag → ExecuteBeadOptions path.
40		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
41	
42		// Sanity-check: the command itself succeeded.
43		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
44		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
45		assert.Equal(t, "test-provider", res.Provider)
46		assert.Equal(t, "test-model-7b", res.Model)
47	
48		// Read routing events directly from the bead store that runAgentExecuteBead
49		// created (backed by f.WorkingDir/.ddx). This verifies end-to-end persistence,
50		// not just that appendBeadRoutingEvidence was called in isolation.
51		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, ".ddx"))
52		routingEvents, err := store.EventsByKind("my-bead", "routing")
53		require.NoError(t, err)
54		require.Len(t, routingEvents, 1, "expected exactly one kind:routing event")
55	
56		evt := routingEvents[0]
57		assert.Equal(t, "routing", evt.Kind)
58		assert.Equal(t, "ddx", evt.Actor)
59		assert.Equal(t, "ddx agent execute-bead", evt.Source)
60		assert.NotEmpty(t, evt.Summary)
61	
62		// Parse the JSON body and assert all three required routing fields.
63		var body struct {
64			ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
65			ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
66			RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
67			BaseURL          string `json:"base_url"`
68		}
69		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(evt.Body), &body),
70			"routing event body should be valid JSON: %s", evt.Body)
71		assert.Equal(t, "test-provider", body.ResolvedProvider)
72		assert.Equal(t, "test-model-7b", body.ResolvedModel)
73		assert.Equal(t, "catalog-match", body.RouteReason)
74		assert.Equal(t, "http://test.example.com/v1", body.BaseURL)
75	}

23	// fakeExecuteBeadGit is a mock implementation of executeBeadGitOps for tests.
24	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
25		mu sync.Mutex
26	
27		// mainHeadRev is returned by HeadRev/ResolveRev for the main working dir.
28		mainHeadRev string
29		// headRevSeq, when set, is returned in order for successive main-dir HeadRev calls.
30		headRevSeq []string
31		headRevIdx int
32		// wtHeadRev is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths (after agent run).
33		wtHeadRev string
34		// wtDirty is returned by IsDirty for worktree paths.
35		wtDirty bool
36		// synthRev, if set, is applied as wtHeadRev when SynthesizeCommit is called.
37		synthRev string
38		// wtHeadRevErr, if set, is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths.
39		wtHeadRevErr error
40		dirty        bool
41		mergeErr     error
42		updateRefErr error
43	
44		addedWTs   []string
45		addedWTRev string
46		removedWTs []string
47		refs       map[string]string // ref -> sha recorded by UpdateRef
48		worktrees  []string          // paths returned by WorktreeList
49	
50		mergeCalls int
51		mergeRev   string
52	}
53	
54	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
55		f.mu.Lock()
56		defer f.mu.Unlock()
57		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
58			if f.wtHeadRevErr != nil {
59				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
60			}
61			return f.wtHeadRev, nil
62		}
63		if len(f.headRevSeq) > 0 {
64			idx := f.headRevIdx
65			if idx >= len(f.headRevSeq) {
66				idx = len(f.headRevSeq) - 1
67			}
68			rev := f.headRevSeq[idx]
69			f.headRevIdx++
70			return rev, nil
71		}
72		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
73	}
74	
75	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
76		f.mu.Lock()
77		defer f.mu.Unlock()
78		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
79	}
80	
81	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error) {
82		f.mu.Lock()
83		defer f.mu.Unlock()
84		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
85			return f.wtDirty, nil
86		}
87		return f.dirty, nil
88	}
89	
90	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error {
91		f.mu.Lock()
92		defer f.mu.Unlock()
93		f.addedWTs = append(f.addedWTs, wtPath)
94		f.addedWTRev = rev
95		if err := os.MkdirAll(wtPath, 0o755); err != nil {
96			return err
97		}
98		beadFile := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")
99		if _, err := os.Stat(beadFile); err == nil {
100			if err := copyTestFile(beadFile, filepath.Join(wtPath, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")); err != nil {
101				return err
102			}
103		}
104		docsDir := filepath.Join(dir, "docs")
105		if _, err := os.Stat(docsDir); err == nil {
106			if err := copyTree(docsDir, filepath.Join(wtPath, "docs")); err != nil {
107				return err
108			}
109		}
110		return nil
111	}
112	
113	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error {
114		f.mu.Lock()
115		defer f.mu.Unlock()
116		f.removedWTs = append(f.removedWTs, wtPath)
117		if err := os.RemoveAll(wtPath); err != nil {
118			return err
119		}
120		return nil
121	}
122	
123	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error) {
124		f.mu.Lock()
125		defer f.mu.Unlock()
126		return f.worktrees, nil
127	}

130		f.mu.Lock()
131		defer f.mu.Unlock()
132		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) && f.synthRev != "" {
133			f.wtHeadRev = f.synthRev
134			return true, nil
135		}
136		// wtDirty is true but synthRev is empty: simulates all-noise worktree.
137		return false, nil
138	}

141

20	// TestMain clears all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
21	// that tests invoking git commands operate on their own temp-dir repositories
22	// rather than any repository inherited from a hook invocation (e.g. GIT_DIR
23	// set by lefthook during pre-commit). Prefix scrub is important — lefthook
24	// sets GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL, GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS etc. that
25	// also leak into test subprocesses and corrupt the shared repo config.
26	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
27		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
30					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
31				}
32			}
33		}
34		cleanupTemp := isolateCmdTestTempRoot()
35		code := m.Run()
36		cleanupTemp()
37		os.Exit(code)
38	}
39	
40	func isolateCmdTestTempRoot() func() {
41		tempRoot, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-cmd-tests-*")
42		if err != nil {
43			return func() {}
44		}
45		oldTmpDir, hadTmpDir := os.LookupEnv("TMPDIR")
46		oldTmp, hadTmp := os.LookupEnv("TMP")
47		_ = os.Setenv("TMPDIR", tempRoot)
48		_ = os.Setenv("TMP", tempRoot)
49		return func() {
50			if hadTmpDir {
51				_ = os.Setenv("TMPDIR", oldTmpDir)
52			} else {
53				_ = os.Unsetenv("TMPDIR")
54			}
55			if hadTmp {
56				_ = os.Setenv("TMP", oldTmp)
57			} else {
58				_ = os.Unsetenv("TMP")
59			}
60			_ = os.RemoveAll(tempRoot)
61		}
62	}
63	
64	var (
65		testLibraryPath string
66		testLibraryOnce sync.Once
67	)
68	
69	// GetTestLibraryPath returns the absolute path to a bare git repository
70	// that mimics a real upstream library (like GitHub). This is the ONLY way
71	// tests should get the test library path.
72	func GetTestLibraryPath() string {
73		testLibraryOnce.Do(func() {
74			// Find the test fixtures directory
75			_, filename, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
76			cmdDir := filepath.Dir(filename)
77			fixtureDir := filepath.Join(cmdDir, "..", "test", "fixtures", "ddx-library")
78			fixtureDir, _ = filepath.Abs(fixtureDir)
79	
80			// Determine base temp directory: TMP -> TMPDIR -> /tmp
81			tempBase := "/tmp"
82			if tmp := os.Getenv("TMP"); tmp != "" {
83				tempBase = tmp
84			} else if tmpdir := os.Getenv("TMPDIR"); tmpdir != "" {
85				tempBase = tmpdir
86			}
87	
88			workingRepo := filepath.Join(tempBase, ".test-library")
89			bareRepo := filepath.Join(tempBase, ".test-library.git")
90			testLibraryPath = bareRepo
91	
92			// Check if we need to recreate (fixtures changed or doesn't exist)
93			needsRecreate := false
94			if stat, err := os.Stat(bareRepo); err != nil || !stat.IsDir() {
95				needsRecreate = true
96			} else if os.Getenv("CI") == "" {
97				// In local dev, check if any fixture is newer than the repo
98				repoModTime := stat.ModTime()
99				filepath.Walk(fixtureDir, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
100					if err == nil && !info.IsDir() && info.ModTime().After(repoModTime) {
101						needsRecreate = true
102					}
103					return nil
104				})
105			}
106	
107			if needsRecreate {
108				// Clean up old repos
109				os.RemoveAll(workingRepo)
110				os.RemoveAll(bareRepo)
111	
112				// Create working repo and copy fixtures
113				if err := os.MkdirAll(workingRepo, 0755); err != nil {
114					panic(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to create working repo: %v", err))
115				}
116	
117				if err := syncDirectory(fixtureDir, workingRepo); err != nil {
118					panic(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to copy fixtures: %v", err))
119				}
120	
121				// Initialize git repo
122				cmds := []struct {
123					args []string
124					dir  string
125				}{
126					{[]string{"git", "init", "-b", "master"}, workingRepo},
127					{[]string{"git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com"}, workingRepo},
128					{[]string{"git", "config", "user.name", "Test User"}, workingRepo},
129					{[]string{"git", "add", "."}, workingRepo},
130					{[]string{"git", "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "Test fixture"}, workingRepo},
131					{[]string{"git", "init", "--bare", bareRepo}, ""},
132					{[]string{"git", "remote", "add", "origin", bareRepo}, workingRepo},
133					{[]string{"git", "push", "origin", "master"}, workingRepo},
134				}
135	
136				for _, c := range cmds {
137					cmd := exec.Command(c.args[0], c.args[1:]...)
138					if c.dir != "" {
139						cmd.Dir = c.dir
140					}
141					if output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
142						panic(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to run %v: %v\nOutput: %s", c.args, err, output))
143					}
144				}
145			}
146		})
147		return testLibraryPath
148	}
149	
150	// syncDirectory copies files from src to dst, preserving directory structure
151	func syncDirectory(src, dst string) error {
152		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
153			if err != nil {
154				return err
155			}
156	
157			// Skip .git directories from source
158			if info.IsDir() && info.Name() == ".git" {
159				return filepath.SkipDir
160			}
161	
162			// Calculate relative path
163			relPath, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
164			if err != nil {
165				return err
166			}
167	
168			dstPath := filepath.Join(dst, relPath)
169	
170			if info.IsDir() {
171				return os.MkdirAll(dstPath, info.Mode())
172			}
173	
174			// Copy file
175			srcFile, err := os.Open(path)
176			if err != nil {
177				return err
178			}
179			defer func() { _ = srcFile.Close() }()
180	
181			dstFile, err := os.Create(dstPath)
182			if err != nil {
183				return err
184			}
185			defer func() { _ = dstFile.Close() }()
186	
187			if _, err := dstFile.ReadFrom(srcFile); err != nil {
188				return err
189			}
190	
191			return os.Chmod(dstPath, info.Mode())
192		})
193	}
194	
195	// TestEnvironment provides isolated testing environment for .ddx/config.yaml
196	type TestEnvironment struct {
197		Dir            string
198		ConfigPath     string
199		LibraryPath    string
200		TestLibraryURL string
201		GitInitialized bool
202	
203		// Optional customizations
204		Platform     string
205		Architecture string
206		HomeDir      string
207	
208		t *testing.T
209	}
210	
211	// TestEnvOption is a functional option for configuring TestEnvironment
212	type TestEnvOption func(*TestEnvironment)
213	
214	// WithGitInit controls whether to initialize a git repository
215	func WithGitInit(init bool) TestEnvOption {
216		return func(te *TestEnvironment) {
217			te.GitInitialized = init
218		}
219	}
220	
221	// WithPlatform sets the platform and architecture for installation tests
222	func WithPlatform(platform, arch string) TestEnvOption {
223		return func(te *TestEnvironment) {
224			te.Platform = platform
225			te.Architecture = arch
226		}
227	}
228	
229	// WithHomeDir sets a custom home directory
230	func WithHomeDir(homeDir string) TestEnvOption {
231		return func(te *TestEnvironment) {
232			te.HomeDir = homeDir
233		}
234	}
235	
236	// WithCustomLibraryURL sets a custom library repository URL
237	func WithCustomLibraryURL(url string) TestEnvOption {
238		return func(te *TestEnvironment) {
239			te.TestLibraryURL = url
240		}
241	}
242	
243	// NewTestEnvironment creates a clean test environment with temp directory
244	// By default: creates git repo, uses test-library fixture via file:// URL
245	func NewTestEnvironment(t *testing.T, opts ...TestEnvOption) *TestEnvironment {
246		t.Helper()
247	
248		tempDir := t.TempDir()
249		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
250		configPath := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml")
251	
252		te := &TestEnvironment{
253			Dir:            tempDir,
254			ConfigPath:     configPath,
255			LibraryPath:    filepath.Join(ddxDir, "library"),
256			GitInitialized: true, // default: init git
257			t:              t,
258		}
259	
260		// Apply options
261		for _, opt := range opts {
262			opt(te)
263		}
264	
265		// Set default test library URL if not customized
266		if te.TestLibraryURL == "" {
267			te.TestLibraryURL = "file://" + GetTestLibraryPath()
268		}
269	
270		// Create .ddx directory
271		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
272	
273		// Initialize git repository if requested
274		if te.GitInitialized {
275			te.initGit()
276		}
277	
278		return te
279	}
280	
281	// initGit initializes a git repository in the test directory
282	func (te *TestEnvironment) initGit() {
283		te.t.Helper()
284	
285		// Strip inherited git env vars so git commands operate on the test
286		// directory's own repository, not any repository inherited from a
287		// hook or parent process (e.g. GIT_DIR set by lefthook).
288		cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
289	
290		// git init
291		gitInit := exec.Command("git", "init")
292		gitInit.Dir = te.Dir
293		gitInit.Env = cleanEnv
294		require.NoError(te.t, gitInit.Run(), "git init should succeed")
295	
296		// git config user.email
297		gitEmail := exec.Command("git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
298		gitEmail.Dir = te.Dir
299		gitEmail.Env = cleanEnv
300		require.NoError(te.t, gitEmail.Run(), "git config user.email should succeed")
301	
302		// git config user.name
303		gitName := exec.Command("git", "config", "user.name", "Test User")
304		gitName.Dir = te.Dir
305		gitName.Env = cleanEnv
306		require.NoError(te.t, gitName.Run(), "git config user.name should succeed")
307	}
308	
309	// CreateConfig creates a config file with the given content
310	func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateConfig(content string) {
311		te.t.Helper()
312		require.NoError(te.t, os.WriteFile(te.ConfigPath, []byte(content), 0644))
313	}
314	
315	// CreateDefaultConfig creates a minimal valid config file using test library
316	func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateDefaultConfig() {
317		te.t.Helper()
318		content := fmt.Sprintf(`version: "1.0"
319	library:
320	  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
321	  repository:
322	    url: %s
323	    branch: master
324	persona_bindings: {}
325	`, te.TestLibraryURL)
326		te.CreateConfig(content)
327	}
328	
329	// CreateConfigWithCustomURL creates a config with a custom repository URL
330	func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateConfigWithCustomURL(url string) {
331		te.t.Helper()
332		content := fmt.Sprintf(`version: "1.0"
333	library:
334	  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
335	  repository:
336	    url: %s
337	    branch: master
338	persona_bindings: {}
339	`, url)
340		te.CreateConfig(content)
341	}
342	
343	// RunCommand runs a DDx command in the test environment and returns output
344	func (te *TestEnvironment) RunCommand(args ...string) (string, error) {
345		te.t.Helper()
346		factory := NewCommandFactory(te.Dir)
347		cmd := factory.NewRootCommand()
348		cmd.SetArgs(args)
349	
350		var buf bytes.Buffer
351		cmd.SetOut(&buf)
352		cmd.SetErr(&buf)
353	
354		err := cmd.Execute()
355		return buf.String(), err
356	}
357	
358	// InitWithDDx properly initializes DDx in the test environment using ddx init
359	// If git is initialized, creates an initial commit before running ddx init.
360	// Otherwise uses --no-git flag. In CI environments, always uses --no-git.
361	func (te *TestEnvironment) InitWithDDx(flags ...string) {
362		te.t.Helper()
363	
364		// Check if we should skip git operations
365		hasNoGitFlag := false
366		for _, flag := range flags {
367			if flag == "--no-git" {
368				hasNoGitFlag = true
369				break
370			}
371		}
372	
373		// If git is initialized and we're not using --no-git, create initial commit
374		if te.GitInitialized && !hasNoGitFlag {
375			cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
376			te.CreateFile("README.md", "# Test Project")
377			gitAdd := exec.Command("git", "add", ".")
378			gitAdd.Dir = te.Dir
379			gitAdd.Env = cleanEnv
380			require.NoError(te.t, gitAdd.Run())
381			gitCommit := exec.Command("git", "commit", "-m", "Initial commit")
382			gitCommit.Dir = te.Dir
383			gitCommit.Env = cleanEnv
384			require.NoError(te.t, gitCommit.Run())
385		}
386	
387		// Default flags if none provided
388		if len(flags) == 0 {
389			if te.GitInitialized {
390				// Use --repository and --branch flags to specify test library
391				flags = []string{"--repository", te.TestLibraryURL, "--branch", "master", "--silent", "--skip-claude-injection"}
392			} else {
393				// No git, use --no-git flag
394				flags = []string{"--no-git", "--silent", "--skip-claude-injection"}
395			}
396		}
397	
398		args := append([]string{"init"}, flags...)
399		output, err := te.RunCommand(args...)
400		require.NoError(te.t, err, "init should succeed: %s", output)
401	}
402	
403	// LoadConfig loads the config using ConfigLoader
404	func (te *TestEnvironment) LoadConfig() (*config.Config, error) {
405		loader, err := config.NewConfigLoaderWithWorkingDir(te.Dir)
406		if err != nil {
407			return nil, err
408		}
409		return loader.LoadConfig()
410	}
411	
412	// CreateFile creates any file in the test environment
413	func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateFile(relativePath, content string) {
414		te.t.Helper()
415		fullPath := filepath.Join(te.Dir, relativePath)
416		dir := filepath.Dir(fullPath)
417		require.NoError(te.t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755))
418		require.NoError(te.t, os.WriteFile(fullPath, []byte(content), 0644))
419	}
420	
421	// NewTestRootCommand creates a fresh root command for tests using isolated temp directory
422	// This is the preferred way to create test commands - it ensures test isolation.
423	func NewTestRootCommand(t *testing.T) *CommandFactory {
424		t.Helper()
425		tempDir := t.TempDir()
426		return NewCommandFactory(tempDir)
427	}
428	
429	// NewTestRootCommandWithDir creates a test command with a specific working directory
430	// Use this when your test needs to operate in a specific directory.
431	func NewTestRootCommandWithDir(dir string) *CommandFactory {
432		return NewCommandFactory(dir)
433	}
434	
435	// executeCommand is a helper to execute commands with captured output (legacy compatibility)
436	// New tests should use TestEnvironment.RunCommand() instead
437	func executeCommand(root *cobra.Command, args ...string) (output string, err error) {
438		buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
439		root.SetOut(buf)
440		root.SetErr(buf)
441		root.SetArgs(args)
442	
443		err = root.Execute()
444		return buf.String(), err
445	}
