data.text: Let me read the full e2e routing test to understand the migration needed:

data.output:
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"testing"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
12	)
13	
14	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted is an end-to-end integration test that
15	// drives runAgentExecuteBead via a cobra Command.Execute call and asserts that
16	// routing evidence (resolved_provider, resolved_model, route_reason) is persisted
17	// as a kind:routing bead event in the bead store.
18	//
19	// This test exercises the full pipeline:
20	//
21	//	CLI flags → ExecuteBeadOptions → AgentRunner.Run → appendBeadRoutingEvidence → bead store
22	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted(t *testing.T) {
23		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
24			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
25			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222", // agent produced a commit
26			mergeErr:    nil,
27		}
28		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
29			ExitCode:        0,
30			Harness:         "mock-harness",
31			Provider:        "test-provider",
32			Model:           "test-model-7b",
33			RouteReason:     "catalog-match",
34			ResolvedBaseURL: "http://test.example.com/v1",
35		}}
36		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
37	
38		// Drive the full command via cobra Command.Execute — not by calling internal
39		// functions directly. This exercises the CLI flag → ExecuteBeadOptions path.
40		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
41	
42		// Sanity-check: the command itself succeeded.
43		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
44		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
45		assert.Equal(t, "test-provider", res.Provider)
46		assert.Equal(t, "test-model-7b", res.Model)
47	
48		// Read routing events directly from the bead store that runAgentExecuteBead
49		// created (backed by f.WorkingDir/.ddx). This verifies end-to-end persistence,
50		// not just that appendBeadRoutingEvidence was called in isolation.
51		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, ".ddx"))
52		routingEvents, err := store.EventsByKind("my-bead", "routing")
53		require.NoError(t, err)
54		require.Len(t, routingEvents, 1, "expected exactly one kind:routing event")
55	
56		evt := routingEvents[0]
57		assert.Equal(t, "routing", evt.Kind)
58		assert.Equal(t, "ddx", evt.Actor)
59		assert.Equal(t, "ddx agent execute-bead", evt.Source)
60		assert.NotEmpty(t, evt.Summary)
61	
62		// Parse the JSON body and assert all three required routing fields.
63		var body struct {
64			ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
65			ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
66			RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
67			BaseURL          string `json:"base_url"`
68		}
69		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(evt.Body), &body),
70			"routing event body should be valid JSON: %s", evt.Body)
71		assert.Equal(t, "test-provider", body.ResolvedProvider)
72		assert.Equal(t, "test-model-7b", body.ResolvedModel)
73		assert.Equal(t, "catalog-match", body.RouteReason)
74		assert.Equal(t, "http://test.example.com/v1", body.BaseURL)
75	}
76	
77	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceProviderFallsBackToHarness verifies that when
78	// the runner returns an empty Provider, the routing event's resolved_provider
79	// falls back to the harness name (as implemented in appendBeadRoutingEvidence).
80	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceProviderFallsBackToHarness(t *testing.T) {
81		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
82			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
83			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
84		}
85		// Runner returns no Provider — harness should be used as resolved_provider.
86		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
87			ExitCode:    0,
88			Harness:     "fallback-harness",
89			Provider:    "", // empty — triggers harness fallback
90			Model:       "fallback-model",
91			RouteReason: "first-available",
92		}}
93		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
94	
95		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
96		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
97	
98		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, ".ddx"))
99		routingEvents, err := store.EventsByKind("my-bead", "routing")
100		require.NoError(t, err)
101		require.Len(t, routingEvents, 1)
102	
103		var body struct {
104			ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
105			ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
106			RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
107		}
108		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(routingEvents[0].Body), &body))
109		// Provider was empty, so resolved_provider falls back to harness.
110		assert.Equal(t, "fallback-harness", body.ResolvedProvider)
111		assert.Equal(t, "fallback-model", body.ResolvedModel)
112		assert.Equal(t, "first-available", body.RouteReason)
113	}
114	
115	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceNoEvidence verifies that routing evidence is
116	// persisted even when the agent exits without commits or rationale.
117	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceNoEvidence(t *testing.T) {
118		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
119			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
120			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // same rev — no commits made
121			wtDirty:     false,
122		}
123		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
124			ExitCode:    0,
125			Harness:     "mock-harness",
126			Provider:    "no-change-provider",
127			Model:       "no-change-model",
128			RouteReason: "direct-override",
129		}}
130		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
131	
132		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
133		require.Equal(t, "no-evidence", res.Outcome)
134	
135		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, ".ddx"))
136		routingEvents, err := store.EventsByKind("my-bead", "routing")
137		require.NoError(t, err)
138		require.Len(t, routingEvents, 1, "routing event should be persisted even on no-evidence outcome")
139	
140		var body struct {
141			ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
142			ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
143			RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
144		}
145		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(routingEvents[0].Body), &body))
146		assert.Equal(t, "no-change-provider", body.ResolvedProvider)
147		assert.Equal(t, "no-change-model", body.ResolvedModel)
148		assert.Equal(t, "direct-override", body.RouteReason)
149	}

	InputTokens            int     `json:"input_tokens" yaml:"input_tokens"`
	OutputTokens           int     `json:"output_tokens" yaml:"output_tokens"`
	CostUSD                float64 `json:"cost_usd" yaml:"cost_usd"`

	NativeOutputTokens     int     `json:"native_output_tokens,omitempty" yaml:"native_output_tokens,omitempty"`
	NativeTotalTokens      int     `json:"native_total_tokens,omitempty" yaml:"native_total_tokens,omitempty"`
	NativeSessionCount     int     `json:"native_session_count,omitempty" yaml:"native_session_count,omitempty"`
	NativeQuotaUsedPercent int     `json:"native_quota_used_percent,omitempty" yaml:"native_quota_used_percent,omitempty"`
}

type usageAgg struct {
	sessions     int
	inputTokens  int
	outputTokens int
	costUSD      float64
	totalDurMS   int
	costBasis    string
}

type usageSessionRecord struct {
	entry agent.SessionEntry
}

	a.sessions++

	a.outputTokens += entry.OutputTokens
	a.totalDurMS += entry.Duration

	// Use recorded cost if available, else estimate from pricing table.
	if entry.CostUSD > 0 {
		a.costUSD += entry.CostUSD

15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
91		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
92		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
93			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
94		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
95		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98	
99		// Register "ddx work plan" and "ddx work focus" as subcommands of "ddx work".
100		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
101		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
102	
103		return cmd
104	}
105	
106	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
107	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
108	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
109	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
110	// by this layer.
111	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
113			return err
114		}
115	
116		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
120		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
121		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123		if !asJSON {
124			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
125			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
126				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
127			})
128		}
129	
130		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
131	}

func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "agent",
		Short: "Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging",
		Long: `Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

The embedded DDx agent harness is named 'agent' and is always available without
installing external binaries. Use --harness agent or --profile cheap to route to it.

Profile routing (--profile default|cheap|fast|smart) selects the best available harness
and model automatically. Workflow tools should prefer --profile over --harness to
stay decoupled from harness installation details.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --profile default --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --model gpt-5.4   # explicit override; avoid by default
  ddx agent run --harness agent --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities agent
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor

input.description: Read the docs check script

data.output:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
allowlist="$root/docs/dev/legacy-routing-docs-allowlist.tsv"

pattern='\bddx agent (run|execute-bead|execute-loop)\b|--harnesses\b|--quorum\b|--profile\b|profile_ladders|model_overrides|ResolveRoute|RouteDecision'

scan_paths=(
  "docs/agent-execute.md"
  "docs/dev"
  "docs/migrations"
  "docs/helix/00-discover"
  "docs/helix/01-frame"
  "docs/helix/02-design"
  "docs/helix/03-test/test-plans"
  "docs/helix/tests"
  "website/content/docs/cli/commands"
)

if [[ ! -f "$allowlist" ]]; then
  echo "missing allowlist: $allowlist" >&2
  exit 2
fi

validate_allowlist() {
  local row=0
  local path match reason extra
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r path match reason extra || [[ -n "${path:-}" ]]; do
    row=$((row + 1))
    [[ -z "${path:-}" || "${path:0:1}" == "#" ]] && continue
    if [[ -n "${extra:-}" || -z "${path:-}" || -z "${match:-}" || -z "${reason:-}" ]]; then
      echo "invalid allowlist row $row: expected path<TAB>matching text<TAB>reason" >&2
      exit 2
    fi
  done < "$allowlist"
}

is_allowed() {
  local hit_path="$1"
  local hit_text="$2"
  local path match reason

  while IFS=$'\t' read -r path match reason || [[ -n "${path:-}" ]]; do
    [[ -z "${path:-}" || "${path:0:1}" == "#" ]] && continue
    if [[ "$hit_path" == "$path" && "$hit_text" == *"$match"* ]]; then
      return 0
    fi
  done < "$allowlist"

  return 1
}

validate_allowlist

tmp="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT

set +e
(
  cd "$root"
  rg -n --glob '!docs/dev/legacy-routing-docs-allowlist.tsv' -e "$pattern" "${scan_paths[@]}"
) > "$tmp"
rg_status=$?
set -e

if [[ "$rg_status" -eq 1 ]]; then
  exit 0
fi
if [[ "$rg_status" -ne 0 ]]; then
  cat "$tmp" >&2
  exit "$rg_status"
fi

unallowed=0
while IFS=: read -r path line_no text || [[ -n "${path:-}" ]]; do
  [[ -z "${path:-}" ]] && continue
  if ! is_allowed "$path" "$text"; then
    if [[ "$unallowed" -eq 0 ]]; then
      echo "unallowlisted legacy routing docs references:" >&2
    fi
    printf '%s:%s:%s\n' "$path" "$line_no" "$text" >&2
    unallowed=$((unallowed + 1))
  fi
done < "$tmp"

if [[ "$unallowed" -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo >&2
  echo "Add a narrow row to docs/dev/legacy-routing-docs-allowlist.tsv only for historical, migration, or explicit deprecation references." >&2
  exit 1
fi

input.content:
# agent-cmd test migration — ddx-d8474e0e

This document classifies every test in the 4 named `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead*_test.go`
and `agent_execute_loop_test.go` files that were deleted as part of retiring the
`ddx agent` CLI surface. Dispositions: **migrated** (logic ported to a surviving
test), **superseded** (the deleted CLI surface was the only target; deeper coverage
already exists in `cli/internal/agent/`), or **absorbed** (assertion captured by one
of the 5 new AC tests).

---

## agent_execute_bead_test.go (30 tests)

| Test | Disposition | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `TestExecuteBeadMerge` | superseded | Merge path covered by `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_*_test.go`; `ddx try` happy-path merge exercised in `TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes` |
| `TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure` | superseded | Preserve-on-conflict path covered by internal land-conflict tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadNoMerge` | migrated | `--no-merge` / `--preserve` flag verified in `TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough` |
| `TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness` | superseded | Iteration-ref naming tested in internal `execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go` |
| `TestExecuteBeadNoChanges` | superseded | No-changes short-circuit covered by internal agent tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadNoEvidenceProducedWithoutCommitOrRationale` | superseded | Evidence-gating AC lives in internal `execute_bead_artifacts_test.go` |
| `TestExecuteBeadDirtyWorktreeWithoutCommits` | superseded | Internal gate tested in `execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go` |
| `TestExecuteBeadMergePreservesContext` | superseded | Covered by internal merge-context tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadSynthesizesPromptAndArtifacts` | superseded | Prompt-synthesis covered by internal `execute_bead_artifacts_test.go::TestExecuteBead_DeterministicPromptContent` |
| `TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID` | superseded | Path-style ID resolution tested at the internal Worker layer |
| `TestExecuteBeadWritesResultArtifactBundle` | absorbed | `TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead` asserts the full JSON result schema through `ddx try` |
| `TestExecuteBeadFromRevFlag` | migrated | `--from` flag plumbing verified in `TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough` |
| `TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery` | absorbed | `TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn` is the direct AC replacement |
| `TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized` | superseded | Harness-noise filtering covered by internal prompt-synthesis tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits` | superseded | Error-no-commit path covered in internal agent error tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadTimeoutNoCommitsReportsExecutionFailure` | superseded | Timeout path exercised by internal cancel/context tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreservesBeforeLand` | superseded | Covered by internal land-conflict recovery tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves` | superseded | Covered by internal land-conflict recovery tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput` | superseded | Output formatting tested in internal artifacts tests; exit-code contract in `TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2` |
| `TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure` | superseded | HEAD-rev error path covered by internal agent tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure` | superseded | Compound error path covered by internal agent tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID` | migrated | `TestTry_BeadNotFound` covers the not-found guard in `ddx try` |
| `TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields` | absorbed | `TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead` pins full evidence-field schema |
| `TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough` | migrated | `--model` flag passthrough verified in `TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough` |
| `TestExecuteBeadStatusMapping` | superseded | Status→exit mapping covered by `TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2` |
| `TestExecuteBeadGatePass` | superseded | Gate-pass merge path covered by internal gate tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadGateBlocksLanding` | absorbed | `TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding` directly covers gate-context injection and preserve-on-fail |
| `TestExecuteBeadNoGatesWhenNoChanges` | superseded | No-gate short-circuit for no-change runs covered by internal `execute_bead_artifacts_test.go::TestExecuteBead_NoChecksArtifactWhenNoGates` |
| `TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected` | superseded | Embedded-agent stderr redirection covered by internal runner tests |
| `TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged` | superseded | XML-tag assertion lives in internal `execute_bead_artifacts_test.go::TestExecuteBead_DeterministicPromptContent` |

---

## agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go (1 test)

| Test | Disposition | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `TestExecuteBeadContextBudgetFromConfig` | superseded | Fully absorbed by `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_context_budget_test.go` which has 6 targeted context-budget tests covering minimal, default, empty-refs, omit-spec-doc, large-description, and decomposition-rejection cases |

---

## agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go (3 tests)

| Test | Disposition | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `TestExecuteBead_RequiredGatePass_Merges` | absorbed | Gate-pass→merge behavior pinned at the `ddx try` surface by `TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding` |
| `TestExecuteBead_RequiredGateFail_Preserves` | absorbed | Gate-fail→preserve behavior pinned at the `ddx try` surface by `TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding` |
| `TestExecuteBead_NoGoverningIDs_Merges` | superseded | No-governing-IDs merge default covered by internal gate tests (`execute_bead_land_conflict_recover_test.go`) |

---

## agent_execute_loop_test.go (2 tests)

| Test | Disposition | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `TestWorkUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan` | migrated | Project-root no-work scan behavior exercised in `TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue` and `TestWorkStopSummary_DrainedEmptyQueue` in `work_test.go` |
| `TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings` | superseded | JSON-amid-warnings parsing is a `cli/internal/agent` concern; covered by `omlx_e2e_test.go` SSE frame fixtures (the `sseCommentFilter` path) |

---

## Summary

| Disposition | Count |
|---|---|
| absorbed (by a named AC test) | 6 |
| migrated (to a surviving cmd-layer test) | 5 |
| superseded (coverage in cli/internal/agent/) | 25 |
| **Total** | **36** |

go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
--
    go-test-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Full Go test suite"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'

      name: "Bead Schema Guard"
